pos_text: The proximity to the train station was advantageous – it takes about a quarter of an hour to get to the old town.
neg_text:
Events were announced via booking (after booking, mind you). I assume it's a bit louder inside the building itself, but not that the bass is thumping into the room until 1 a.m. so much that the bed vibrates. And quite honestly, I'm a good sleeper and very understanding, but... Such noise levels are unacceptable, especially given the hotel's policy of quiet hours from 10 pm to 6 am. After I asked for a discount, I was turned away rudely - according to the motto: I would have received an email and could have cancelled. My hairdryer in the room didn't work, neither did the access card to the room on Saturday, and I had to wait three-quarters of an hour at 10 pm to get into my room (no compensation was offered here either). The receptionists seemed more overwhelmed and annoyed than solution-oriented towards the guests. Keep your eyes open when choosing a job - I say this as a former hotel manager in what was then Berlin Mitte. I must commend the cleaning service. The lady was very helpful and also very friendly.

I cannot comment on breakfast and the restaurant - the bar was closed due to the parties. I hadn't booked breakfast - I wanted to have breakfast later in town.

title: Conference at the hotel
pos_text:
A conference was held at Novotel. I liked everything. We stayed at the hotel from December 13 to December 16.
The conference room was very comfortable and beautiful, the equipment worked perfectly. It was great that the screen covered the entire wall - it really helped the speakers and looked great.
We also ordered a banquet for 112 people at the same hotel. The banquet hall was decorated very beautifully and we were fed deliciously.
Many thanks to all the staff. You are all very attentive and quickly and effectively solved all the problems that we set before you.
Huge thanks to Albine, she met us at the reception, helped with all the questions regarding the settlement, and settled us all in efficiently. She provided this opportunity to everyone who wanted nearby rooms.
Overall, I liked the hotel; it was beautiful, stylish, and clean.
And the staff is amazing and friendly, I admire all the people who served us.

We also had dinner at the hotel restaurant – the portions could have been a bit larger, but the taste of the dish alone made up for it. The salmon trout was really delicious and fresh.

Another plus is the clean bathroom, decorated in a friendly, modern style. The mattress on the bed was comfortable, which ensured we had a comfortable night's sleep. An additional advantage is the possibility of staying with pets - many people took advantage of this option and there were many guests at the hotel with their pets.

Overall, I recommend this hotel, especially for the friendly service, good food and friendly atmosphere.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The rooms are very nice and clean, which is a huge plus for the entire facility. The room has a safe and toiletries needed for traveling, as well as a refrigerator. The ladies at the reception and in the restaurant are very nice and helpful, very polite.
Very tasty meals in the afternoon buffet open from 4 p.m., we took advantage of it as a family - very large portions, tasty and relatively inexpensive.
neg_text:
Even though the facility is presented as a hotel and recreation/sports center, there is no gym and a PAID swimming pool also for hotel guests, PLN 24 for an adult and an additional PLN 12 for a swimming cap if you do not have one. The lady at the reception desk at the pool wasn't very nice, but I understand that someone can have a bad day.
Breakfasts rated as fantastic leave a lot to be desired. Since the breakfast restaurant was open until 11 a.m. on the weekend, one would expect that items would still be added to the buffet at 9 a.m. Unfortunately, nothing could be further from the truth, at 9:00 one inedible cold cut.
Some of the products were very tasty and fresh, but the buffet also included dried fruit and nuts in glass jars, as well as homemade jams and preserves, which looked very tempting - so my husband and I decided to take advantage of these benefits. Unfortunately, in nuts and dried fruit - the next day we found mold, which is an absolute disqualification for me.

The hotel is ok as a stopover, but the most disappointing thing is the breakfast, which is relatively expensive - for this price you can find a really good quality breakfast place in Wrocław, and in the city center.

title: Peaceful, Nice, Clean, Pleasant
pos_text:
The meeting in Wrocław ran over, so instead of dreaming of the highway at 11 p.m., I ended up at the DIKUL Hotel. Last minute booking – and bingo! A huge room, impeccable cleanliness, and a receptionist who greeted you so kindly that you forgot about the whole day at work.

In the morning – the view from the window is worthy of an advertisement for Wrocław, even though the weather is rather “autumn depression”.

For comparison, I recently slept in a 5-star hotel, The Granary La Suite, for six times the price and… seriously? DIKUL wins with cleanliness and politeness of service.

To sum up: luxury is not just about gold door handles – sometimes it's just a good night's sleep and a sincere smile at the reception desk. I recommend!
neg_text: The bathroom lacks basic things – a toothbrush and toothpaste, shower gel, or even a little cream. When you make a spontaneous booking for one night, you really feel the lack of such details. The hotel has GREAT/HUGE/GIGANTIC potential, but it feels like it's missing the right direction. Looking at this hotel, I see a place that – with appropriate development and consistent management – can easily compete with premium facilities. All it takes is a little strategic approach to customer experience to make DIKUL your number one choice.

This is the first time I've written a negative review about a hotel and I travel a lot. I'm sorry to have to write this, especially since I've used the hotel's services before and had no complaints.
On the day of check-in, we arrived at the hotel around 6 p.m. After receiving the keys and opening the room, we were immediately hit by the stench of the bathroom sewage system. We reported this fact immediately to the hotel reception but unfortunately all we heard was that there was no one to take care of it and that we would not be given another room. We were told that we could only write a complaint on the hotel form, which of course we did. I also asked for a refund, we wanted to stay overnight in another hotel but I was also told there was no possibility of receiving a refund.
To date, and some time has passed, we have not received a response to the complaint, and I would also like to mention that I have not received an invoice for the service (I reported this when booking the hotel). It's a shame that hotel guests are being ignored like this, because we would probably use the services of this hotel more than once, and after this situation we definitely won't do so and we advise others not to do so.

title: The most disappointing thing is cheating the customer and posting false photos
pos_text: BEWARE OF THIS PLACE, it's a scam, the room does not match the photos posted by booking.com
neg_text:
The room is completely inconsistent with the offer. Instead of a King-size bed – a regular, small bed. The room looked completely different than in the photos – it was ugly, neglected, and full of dust. The worst thing, however, was the intense, unpleasant smell – reminiscent of sewers – which persisted throughout the stay and was difficult to bear. We also received a towel with holes.

The photos included in the offer were misleading – they showed a completely different, much better room. The hotel did not offer a room change or agree to a refund. I was forced to spend my entire stay there despite the terrible conditions.
After I asked the reception why I didn't get the room I paid for (approx. PLN 350 per night) The lady initially said that they no longer had these rooms available, but after a while she changed her mind and said that the photos were only illustrative. I wonder why the hotel doesn't put real photos on the booking page instead of illustrative ones, which are inaccurate anyway?

It is also worth remembering that cleaning is paid - PLN 25. The hotel does not include cleaning in the price. I didn't find this information on booking, but I read it at the reception desk.

I definitely do not recommend it - a huge disappointment and a waste of money.

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
Staying at the Radisson in Wrocław was a pure pleasure. From the moment you enter, you feel calm and professional, and the staff truly pays attention to every detail. After visiting the Rotunda and viewing the Racławice Panorama, it was great to relax in the hotel gym.
In the morning, a delicious breakfast awaited us – fresh products, aromatic coffee and a smile from the staff for a good start. This is a place where the day starts better than it ends – and that's exactly why you want to come back.

title: Jin Jiang - swept away
pos_text: I have a great fondness for the Radisson Hotel, as I was one of its first guests back in 2006. As the first 5-star hotel in Wrocław, Radisson has focused on the highest level of customer service from the very beginning. The rooms were spacious, comfortable, and the service so exceptional that every night spent there remained in the memory as something truly special. This experience shaped my expectations of luxury and quality of service for a long time – and I still remember it with great fondness.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, today the hotel no longer represents the same standard. It is clearly visible that the facility is neglected and the staff expects guests to treat the hotel as if it were a former top 5*, even though times have changed and many much more exclusive facilities have been built in the area.

Management remained stuck in the Radisson glory days, failing to keep up with new trends in hospitality. Unfortunately, it is also clear that the entire network is conservative and resistant to change. As a result, today's Radisson hotels have largely ceased to be synonymous with 5* luxury, becoming rather a chain for corporate clients organizing conferences, offering a correct standard, but far from the former excitement and exceptional guest experience.

title: So-so

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
The stay at this hotel turned out to be a bull's eye. From the moment you walk in, you are greeted by professional, smiling staff who immediately make you feel welcome. The room is comfortable, clean and well-equipped, and a morning with the view from the window combined with a cup of coffee is something I will never forget.
The hotel restaurant made a huge impression on me. This is a place where cuisine meets true passion. The dishes were refined in every detail – from taste to presentation. Dinner in such a setting is an experience that can easily be compared to visiting the best restaurants in the city.
Breakfast is another advantage – fresh, varied and served in such a way that people start the day with pleasure. Additionally, a big plus is the convenient parking, which saves a lot of stress.
This hotel is not just a place to stay, but a complete experience – from taste, through comfort, to atmosphere. It was here that I relaxed, recharged my batteries, and learned that attention to detail really makes a difference. This was my 3rd time at this hotel :)

title: Beautiful hotel in a quiet area!
pos_text:
Perfect location. Within walking distance of the centre and certainly far away from the hustle and bustle of the market square.
Very neat rooms and a wonderful breakfast. Friendly staff. The hotel also has a very good restaurant with refined dishes.
neg_text:
The shower head could definitely be a little bigger. I understand that the chain wants to prevent water wastage, but please don't exaggerate.
Some staff members (except reception) have difficulty understanding English.
It is a fine and beautiful hotel, but you also pay a hefty price for it.

title: A successful stay, no comments.

neg_text: Nothing comes to mind.

title: I slept like a king, only without a crown
pos_text:
The room was comfortable, the bed so soft that I began to wonder if I should leave everything and move in permanently.
Breakfast? A festival of flavors and moral dilemmas – is it appropriate to eat the fourth pancake? (It fell out). Service? So nice that for a moment I thought they had mistaken me for someone important. Some kind of minister of convenience or ambassador of scrambled eggs.

The location is also excellent – close to everything, but far enough away not to hear people shouting “where are the dwarves?!”

To sum up: Radisson Blu Wrocław is a hotel where you can feel like a VIP for a moment… without having to sell a kidney. I come back at every opportunity, unless they stop me – e.g. at breakfast.

A hotel full of contradictions. On the one hand, the service is very pleasant and helpful, but on the other hand, in contrast, some elements are very weak.
Inflexible standards within the hotel, and it all started with getting an invoice. Then breakfasts, which are very good, but end at 10:00, with the emphasis on end. The curtain is drawn and the staff eats breakfast. This is the first time I have encountered such a custom, usually hotels are flexible with their guests. Moreover, on Sunday there is not even a moment longer for breakfast, because the curtain is drawn again, where the food is placed and the staff eats. A very strange standard, especially with that curtain. It looked as if the food was to be left for the hotel staff to prevent the guests from eating it all. There is no longer any breakfast on Sunday. On the last day there was a wedding reception until the morning and the morning staff was extremely tense and tense. The staff said things to us that should never have been said to a customer. The words and tone of voice were unacceptable at all, and even more so in a 4* hotel. After check in I was asked if the room should be cleaned, I confirmed that it was. Unfortunately, it wasn't, and we weren't brought any water to make up for the lack. It was only done at my request. For such a high price for accommodation I would expect a better standard. I recommend the hotel, but please work on the hotel standards, because it is 4*.

neg_text:
We arrived at the hotel a little earlier (1 hour) due to a cancelled client appointment. Check It was not possible; we were left sitting in the lobby for an hour with the explanation that the rooms were not ready.

When we saw the room, we wondered what had been done to clean it. (Pictures)
Dust in the corners and long black hairs in the sink.

The wellness area is very outdated; there are ant nests in the relaxation area.
When asked, staff said that they had already informed management, but nothing would be done (see pictures). One can also see from the picture of the sauna rules that things are being done extremely carelessly here.
When I asked to speak to the manager, I was informed that he was not in the building.
Shortly after my request, the ant nests were vacuumed up, but two hours later they were back!

The sauna is supposed to be usable until 10 pm. We were there at 3 pm, everything was fine, the sauna temperature was 90 degrees. When we wanted to go for another sauna session at 7 pm, the sauna heater was already switched off, and the sauna only had a temperature of 60 degrees!

Unfortunately, I have a lot of reservations:
1. First and foremost - I am allergic, I chose this hotel primarily for the sauna, because I really need it during this period. At the reception I was informed that the sauna is available from 7 a.m. I was there at 8:30. the wellness area was not cleaned, the saunas were cold, and the jacuzzi too. I had to leave after an hour to make it to the meeting. By this time the sauna had heated up to 40 degrees, so I was unable to use this facility. Around 9:00 a.m. the cleaning lady came, washed the windows, and scrubbed the floor with a bucket. I understand that such things are the norm in public swimming pools, but not in a hotel of this class in the wellness area (!). You can't relax in such conditions. A huge minus and this situation alone makes me regret my choice and if I had to choose a hotel again, I would definitely not choose this one.
2. Breakfast was mediocre, typical hotel breakfast, weak coffee, dry cakes. There was prosecco available at the entrance, it looked like the staff were going to pour it but they probably didn't feel like it, it was in a cooler with water - melted ice, it was warm. The tables were not cleaned, we could feel crumbs from the previous guests under our hands. The gentleman from the service suggested dishes to order. We ordered poached eggs - they were prepared incorrectly, with way too much vinegar, it was impossible to eat. We went out to the terrace for coffee and dessert, but it was not cleaned up there either, and the umbrellas were not open despite the strong sun.
3. The service here was also a huge disappointment, unfortunately. I arrived with a pregnant woman, no one asked if they could help us, no one held any doors, no one even showed the slightest interest in us. When leaving, we were informed about the cost of 80 PLN for parking, which we had not received any information about beforehand, the person at the reception did not even ask how our stay was, basically took our cards, issued an invoice and said "Goodbye" without even a smile, anything, as if we were making a problem of taking advantage of the offer.

pos_text: Massage. It's hard to find anything more.
neg_text: Complete drama. I don't really know who gave it 5 stars. From the very beginning (basically before your stay) problems started… The massage appointments had to be rescheduled half an hour earlier because even though the spa is open until 10pm, the massage had to end at 9:30pm, which means I had to cut the massage in half because We came from the route straight for a massage. The lady at the reception, Maria or Daria, couldn't quite cope with the invoice. And I was informed that I would not receive an invoice, only when I asked to contact the manager, the invoice was made in 45 seconds. But it was possible! The rooms show signs of time, but are relatively comfortable. We were there in a very hot weather, so the air conditioning was not coping well. The breakfast was quite modest (it's hard to say it's five-star - I recommend going to a five-star hotel and checking what they serve). The same lady at the reception who refused to issue an invoice told me that we had to use the parking ticket until the end of our trip. Upon departure, it turned out that the ticket did not work. There was a traffic jam behind us (the parking lot is so narrow that it's difficult for a larger car to pass), despite the information that we should call the phone number, no one answered. We had to go to the reception and wait again, because when there is checkout in the morning, one slow person is enough. To sum up, the hotel might not have left such bad memories, but the service at every stage was dramatic. To be replaced. We didn't just use the restaurant… I'm afraid of what we would "find" there 🤣 if you have the option of choosing another hotel, even with four stars, I recommend choosing 4 stars.

Breakfast has a wide selection, from hotel classics to pancakes and waffles with all sorts of toppings (so even the smallest kids can eat something), as well as cakes and cookies. Room service is basically unnoticeable, while breakfast, reception and bar service are exactly as they should be.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
We had the misfortune of sleeping in two rooms. The first one had an uncomfortable mattress, the room was rather classic, nothing fancy, but also without any major flaws. There was nothing more than we expected from a hotel. However, on the first day our toilet flush did not work. We reported it and received a response that someone would come to us. We were there for work, so we were out of the room all day. After returning, (around midnight) we saw that the flush still didn't work. So we called the reception desk, and the gentleman there was completely pissed off at us, he told us he was very sorry, but there was nothing he could do because it was Friday evening and the technician didn't work on weekends, and he would suggest changing the room. However, the next morning we would have to go back to the old room with a broken flush (so the room change would only be for the night). As compensation, he offered us breakfast, but we had already purchased it, and free parking for one day (we didn't come by car). We decided to go down to the reception to talk to the gentleman again. The gentleman at the reception desk from Friday to Saturday showed such a lack of professionalism that we were completely taken aback. I am leaving aside the basic rules of communication, such as: It would be better to stand up instead of sitting with your legs spread apart, as you would when talking to another person, but he still stuck to what he had said earlier on the phone. We refused to change rooms for the night, because we least need the toilet at night and it would cause too much confusion.
The next morning, we noticed another gentleman at the reception desk, very nice and polite (actually, the entire staff, apart from one gentleman on night duty from Friday to Saturday, was very nice and polite, helpful, and even supportive, which was a pleasant surprise). Ultimately, it turned out that it was possible to exchange the room, with a working flush.

title: absolutnie nie polecam - fatalny stosunek jakości do ceny
pos_text: Jedyny pozytyw to urozmaicone i smaczne śniadanie
neg_text:
Po pierwsze estetyka korytarzy - wykładzina wypalona prawdopodobnie środkami chemicznymi wyglądała jak z taniego hostelu. Horror.
Po drugie - winda z odrapanymi poręczami i z wytarta instrukcją według której przed wybraniem piętra należy skorzystać z karty klucza; brak oznaczeń pietra na którym winda się zatrzymuje.
Po trzecie informacja - po rezerwacji , dzwoniąc i pytając o możliwość parkingu - uzyskałem informację, że parking jest odległy o 350 m i kosztuje 120 zł, więc zorganizowałem parking w inny sposób. Przy zameldowaniu okazało się, że jednak jest dostępny inny parking za 40 zł (tej informacji nie otrzymałem gdy pytałem podczas rozmowy telefonicznej).
Po czwarte - środki czystości w łazience. Nie mam zarzutów do ogólnej czystości, ale użyte środki w łazience sprawiały, że obuwie kleiło się nieprzyjemnie do podłogi.
Po piąte - ładny efekt doświetlający w łazience - ścianę stanowiły przeszklone drzwi, które prowadzą na wspólny dla kilku pokoi balkon, ale .... folia matowa na jednym z okien niestarannie doklejona, co sprawiało, że stojąc na balkonie wieczorem, przy zaświeconym świetle w łazience - stojąca osoba na balkonie (przypominam balkon wspólny) ma doskonały widok na osobę korzystającą z łazienki i jeśli ktoś nie jest ekshibicjonistą to jest to duży dyskomfort.
Nie będę już dodawał że na pytanie na basenie - czy są dostępne ręczniki - odpowiedź - "jak nie wiszą to nie ma".

title: Péssima experiência, não recomendo a ninguém.
pos_text: Nada.
neg_text:
Fizemos o check-in pelas 20 horas, foi nos dado o quarto 305, deixamos os nossos pertences no mesmo e fomos passear pela cidade. Apenas quando regressámos, pelas 00:00 horas, e dirigiamo-nos para dormir, deparamo-nos que a cama encontrava-se partida em várias zonas, com diversas tábuas de madeira fora da sua posição e os plásticos de suporte às mesmas partidos. Posto isto, alertamos de imediato a recepcionista que se encontrava de serviço a essa hora (não foi a mesma que realizou o nosso check-in) e tiramos fotos para comprovar o mesmo. A recepcionista veio então ao nosso quarto e confirmou o estado da mesma. Posto isto, pedimos então que nos fosse apresentada uma alternativa (mudar de quarto ou então substituírem a cama), o que nos foi dada uma resposta negativa, pois segundo a recepcionista o hotel encontrava-se lotado e não havia mais nenhum quarto livre, sendo a única alternativa reembolsar o dinheiro. Como é obvio, demonstramos o nosso descontentamento, pois a uma hora tão tardia não era conveniente trocar de hotel e pedimos o formulário para fazer a reclamação, este que foi lido e assinado pela recepcionista.
Tivemos então infelizmente de pernoitar nesse quarto com a cama partida, e na manhã do dia seguinte abandonamos o hotel. A recepcionista que se encontrava de serviço era mesma que realizou o nosso check-in do dia anterior, e então decidimos informa-lá também da situação, ao que ela respondeu que supostamente seriamos contactados pelo hotel (?), e se caso não fossemos, forneceu-nos um email para que possamos enviar a nossa reclamação.
Para que não fosse suficiente, o quarto encontrava-se também com um nível de limpeza horrível, cheio de teias de aranha nas paredes e no teto e um cheiro nauseabundo (uma espécie de cheiro a peido/esgoto) vindo da casa de banho, a agua do chuveiro não tinha pressão e um barulho incomodativo (uma espécie de objetos a cair etc…) no corredor logo pela manhã do dia seguinte.

neg_text:
My partner and I decided to treat ourselves and booked one of the larger suites at Hotel Dikul. However, upon arrival we were checked into an small double room just off reception on the ground floor. It was clearly a room
for people with accessibility issues. The room was dated and, compared to the suite we booked, was considerably smaller. It looked nothing like the suite advertised on booking.com’s site. I checked my booking to confirm that I had in fact booked the other room, and I had. We went to reception to request a room move. We were told there were no other rooms. I asked to speak with the manger… they weren’t around. The person on reception looked very young and was extremely unhelpful. I was told that was the room I had booked, which it clearly wasn’t. There was no acknowledgment or apology whatsoever. I asked for a refund if I wasn’t able to stay in the room I had booked. All I was given was a complaints form to hand write. Thankfully our stay was only for 1 night. Upon checkout we were asked if we enjoyed our stay. We explained the situation to the new receptionist who proceeded to sit silently and stare at us. An apology really would have gone a long way. All I can think and hope is that they aren’t used to people not enjoying their stay, which may be reassuring to others I guess. For us the customer service was truly terrible. A week or so after our stay I was emailed a discount offer for my next booking at hotel dikul!! Needless to say I would never ever stay there again. Also to note that the hotel isn’t actually on the market square. It’s close to the market
Square and is still in a convenient location, but we felt the name on here is a little misleading.

Bad smell from the bathroom, barrier - free again, dirty sheets, dirt on the floor, dirty remote controler from AC. We decided to leave the hotel, because it was not really what we ordered. We asked for parking refund, we parked only for 1 - 1,5 hour. There was another lady, maybe they changed shifts, so I informed her again about the problems. She told me that she is new employee so she doesn’t know what to do with the refund, so she called some colleague to help her. The summary was that unfortunately she cannot refund and gave me only some kind of complaint form, which I filled of course. I asked for a manager but there was no one on site, then I asked for manager email so I can contact her/him,but I received only a hotel email, which is not valid in such a situation.
The result is, that the hotel is ignoring my e-mails, no one is responding and every time I call them they tell me that manager is not working. I donť know, but this is just not professional at all.
I am badly dissapointed, because they are not able to even refund me for the parking! I know I paid for non refundable reservation but in such a matter you should fully refund. I am also attaching real photos.

I wholeheartedly recommend this hotel if you don't want to be disappointed like we were. We arrived exhausted after traveling halfway across the country to the Christmas market. We had specifically booked a king room to have more space, but what we found there left us stunned. The photos presented on the website have no relation to reality, it's not about a different furniture arrangement or a differently made bed - THE ENTIRE ROOM WAS 3 CLASSES BELOW WHAT WE SIGNED FOR. We were greeted by an unpleasant smell, the entire space was filled with rickety furniture, and on the bed were two unmade duvets covered in dust and HAIR! There was also hair all over the scratched floor. The string of the large roller blind was broken, so the only thing covering the windows were dirty curtains. The walls were scratched and scuffed, and in the bathroom we were greeted by cobwebs and a spider on them.

The longer we stayed there, the more we felt cheated and finally we decided not to stay in this hotel any longer, so we reported it to Booking and moved to the JAZZ hotel, 4 minutes away, where we actually felt like we were in a hotel, and the illustrative photos are not misleading.

THIS IS DEFINITELY NOT A 200 PLN PER NIGHT HOTEL, AT LEAST 50 PLN.

I will just add that the only thing we have recovered so far is PLN 60 and the photos showing the King room on the hotel booking page are photos of a 4-person room. Below I am attaching the actual appearance of the King room of the "hotel" dikul ;)

Some of the reception staff were smiling, while others seemed quite annoyed by our presence (so unkind that they didn't even explain or give us the note with the Wi-Fi password on it). But the worst thing was the bathroom. We reported that the sink handle was spinning almost 360 degrees and needed to be repaired, and it was also impossible to take a shower without flooding the bathroom. After just 2 minutes, water was pouring out of the shower (you can see from the photos that it's actually a walk-in shower, so the slope isn't right, it floods everything) and it was impossible to keep the water running for even 5 minutes. Hostel-like sheets, small and uncomfortable pillows, and there were no bathroom products (shampoo or shower gel). I think paying almost 150 euros a night to then have these terrible services is unacceptable. If you find a room for 60 euros a night, that's fine...but if not, avoid it and go somewhere else. I wouldn't go back.

There is no air conditioning or any ventilation in the rooms, and on top of that there is an unpleasant smell inside...

We ran away from it immediately after breakfast, because on top of everything else there was the nasty, musty smell of the old room.

The bathroom ventilation only worked for a minute or two after being turned on (so you would have to turn it on every now and then), but it didn't really give anything anyway. The toilet is cramped and uncomfortable.

The shower and sink drains are severely clogged.
The water pressure in the shower head, despite different times of showering, was constantly very, very weak, it was difficult to rinse the shampoo out properly.

No sockets by the beds, TV smaller than the advertising screens in supermarkets. Not that we need it, but it does complete the picture.

The cleaning crew does their job well, and the cleanliness is generally okay, but the facility is definitely showing signs of age and really needs a refresh.

It is clear that the rooms have long since passed their glory days. There is no kettle in the room.
My worst rating was for the restaurant where breakfast was served. There was practically no choice, just watered down scrambled eggs, bread, jam and maybe you could grab some tomato. The photo showing the restaurant's buffet does not reflect what you will find there, because most of these items are not available on an everyday basis.
The staff is also not very interested in replenishing missing items on a regular basis or cleaning the tables. One day we even managed to eat breakfast while watching an employee pick her nose.

title: It was ok, but I wouldn't go back again

The rooms were clean.

neg_text:
There was a party at the hotel on Friday and Saturday. First night at 3. It was impossible to sleep on the second floor even with earplugs in - the whole building was vibrating. Around two in the morning it got quiet. The second night we were moved to the 6th floor. floor and you could hear the party there, but you could fall asleep without a hitch. The hotel warned us about this when we made the reservation, but until you experience it, you don't want to believe it. They don't understand - it's either a party place or a hotel. The receptionist's actions were in the sense of "it's not us, it's DJ, we can't be responsible for it". At least they moved us.
You cannot sleep on the extra bed (stretched couch) - the padding is about 1 cm high.
On Sunday morning, some tables were missing at the breakfast (probably left over for the party) and there was not enough room for all the guests.

During our stay there was a party at the hotel - we were informed that it might be loud, but it was not the party guests who were the problem. Music from the restaurant (2 floors down) it penetrated through the empty walls, you could easily guess the songs :)

title: Hotel for the patient. The hotel's 3 stars are a misunderstanding. Not for this money.
pos_text: On the plus side, the location is great (11/10), perfect for exploring the city on foot. Tasty breakfasts, quite a wide selection, good coffee. Elevator. Aesthetically decorated hotel. The bathroom is fine.
neg_text: At first glance, it might seem that the hotel is OK, but the details "put it down" my stay. When I got there, it turned out that I was not assigned the room I requested (not facing the street), in addition, a broken fan was constantly humming in the room and could not be turned off. Here everything was saved, because they changed my room - not facing the street and with a working fan (or so I thought, because later it turned out that, for a change, it was hardly blowing at all). It seemed that it would be 9-10/10 now, unfortunately what immediately caught my attention was the internal door in my room, as you can easily guess - connecting my room with another one. While the first night the adjacent room seemed empty, unfortunately on the second night, as expected, the loud family watching TV around 1 a.m. of the neighbors outside the door prevented me from sleeping properly. Perhaps it wouldn't have been audible if our rooms had been separated by a wall, but unfortunately, as we've already established, it was the door :). Luckily they were at least closed,.. Since it was the last night of my stay, I gave up on another conversation with the staff, but what kind of standard is this at this price in a hotel? A completely incomprehensible situation. I booked in advance. In turn, the previous day, when I was returning from sightseeing, my room card suddenly stopped working and they simply replaced it without a word of explanation? Poor communication from staff. The gentleman didn't speak to me at all, while one of the ladies spoke so quickly and unclearly that I had trouble understanding her... Are guests a sad necessity? It is also worth mentioning the wonderful green "wardrobes" for clothes that are so narrow that the clothes have to stick out through those funny "windows" like Nussbeisser chocolate-covered nuts.

During the three days of our stay, the room was never tidied, the three towels were never changed, and the bathroom was not cleaned, even the toilet brush was missing.
Using the bathtub (there is no shower cubicle) is uncomfortable as there is no support to hold on to.
In past years I had already frequented other hotels of the Campanile chain and had always had a very good experience, but this time I was disappointed.
At this price you can find better.

title: Location is ok and that's all
pos_text: The location is very good, parking is paid, but we got a space without any problems. The appearance of the room is correct, standard. A very nice view.
neg_text:
Breakfasts are served until 10:30, but unfortunately there was nothing on the plate at 9:30. Literally three mini rolls, no cheese, no lettuce and many other things. The waitresses, instead of checking what was missing, preferred to giggle in the kitchen that, as a great favor, after being noticed, they started clearing the tables that should have been cleared long ago after the guests had left. The lady didn't even wipe our table, it was sticky, there were crumbs all over the table. I think that the ladies have absolutely no idea even the basics of customer service. Breakfast was very modest, with very little variety. Moreover, to make coffee or tea, we have to stand behind the heads of the guests who have a table next to us. Very uncomfortable. Dangerously. The coffee machine is placed in a very bad location. Same thing with juices. I had to apologize to other guests if I could pour juice so as not to disturb them while they were having breakfast, the juices were placed high, almost above their heads. The first section of tables and chairs is ok, but in the back of the room there are armchairs whose glory days have long since passed. It's a shame that guests have to sit on armchairs whose leatherette is falling off and is all cracked. The restaurant is closed on Sundays. However, the nice bartender suggested the dish of the day, because the hotel was serving the group, otherwise it would not have been possible to eat anything in the restaurant. We waited a very long time for the dish of the day, namely: cream of tomato soup - be careful with penne pasta, and for the second course the same penne pasta (overcooked, to boot) with chicken and spinach. Poorly.
In addition, the heating kept turning off and the radiator in the bathroom was always cold. A bathtub with a glass cover that was broken. One can of still water in a double room. Tea and coffee, sugar and creamerfor coffee in the amount that the cat cried, in a room for two days for two people.

Możliwe że polityka firmy jest taka , natomiast reakcja personalu w obiekcie na pytanie dlaczego jest taka sytuacja była opryskliwa i bardzo niemiła . Nawet jeśli nie mają na to wpływu uprzejmość jest mile widziana . Generalnie obsługa poniżej klasy obiektu , parking który zazwyczaj w hotelach Puro jest swoistym rodzajem wyzwaniem dla kierowców , ciasny z okropnym wjazdem i mikroskopijnymi miejscami przy każdym wyjeździe wymagał powrotu do recepcji bo karta niby powinna działać ale tak nie było . Z kartą do pokoju podobnie . Tablet w pokoju żyje swoim życiem . Nie mam problemu z funkcjonowaniem w świecie technologii , jednak ustawienie klimatyzacji kilka razy powracało do ustawień bazowych mimo zadanej każdorazowo temperatury , w nocy dwukrotnie wymagało ponownego ustawiania ponieważ był wyjątkowy upał w pokoju ciężko było wytrzymać . Inne funkcje tabletu też działają tak sobie , pomijając fakt iż menu przywitało osobę o innych danych niż moje i mimo interwencji w recepcji tak pozostało do końca . Po raz pierwszy pobyt to wielkie rozczarowanie , zazwyczaj wyjeżdżałem w euforii z chęcią powrotu i rekomendowałem hotel wśród znajomych , tym razem sytuacja była zupełnie inna . Zdziwienie wzbudza również brak stacji ładowania pojazdów elektrycznych w garażu , w hotelu który jest wysoko oceniany i doba kosztuje 800 zł chyba można oczekiwać wysokich standardów

title: No clue why this hotel is highly rated on Booking
pos_text: The location is great. The rooms inside were clean.
neg_text:
- MOST IMPORTANTLY: the AC DOESN'T WORK PROPERLY; we were there when the temperature outside was about 26-27 degrees C (so not even as high as it gets), but it was practically impossible to have the temperature in the room get below 24 degrees C; as you may see in the point below, it seems to be the case for all the rooms; we ended up swapping hotels after one night because of it
- the stuff: while the stuff was very kind and helpful in standard situations (showing us to the room, check-in, etc.), when there appeared an issue (AC not working), they were not helpful and quite unprofessional; when we complained about the A/C, one staffer told us that ""it was normal that the temperature in the room could not be further decreased, as the day was sunny and the windows in the room were very large", another said that "the A/C system is just different from other hotels", yet another offered us a different room and after our requests and inquiries has assured us that she's verified it personally and that the other room definitely had a properly working AC, so my husband has packed all our things and moved them to that room (on a different floor) just to discover that in that room the A/C had an even bigger problem: it was not even possible to turn it on;
- cleanliness: although the rooms were clean, the corridor leading to them and the lift were not; the carpets were very stained
- room setup: there was this strange setup where the bathroom was divided from the room by a glass wall; we didn't understand the idea to begin with (as probably it's also more costly to furnish), however, it could be done in an acceptable way (that we've seen in a different hotel) with adding a light-blocking curtain to the setup.

title: Basic but nice.
pos_text:
The highlight of my stay was undoubtedly the hotel's exceptional customer service. The staff members were not only friendly but also went out of their way to assist with any inquiries or requests I had. Their genuine willingness to help created a welcoming atmosphere that enhanced my overall stay.

The cleanliness of the hotel was another notable aspect. The attention to detail in maintaining a spotless environment did not go unnoticed. Whether it was the lobby, the corridors, or my room, everything was impeccably clean, contributing to a sense of comfort and well-being.

The comfort of the bed was exceptional. After a day of exploring the city, sinking into the plush mattress was a blissful experience. The quality of the bedding and pillows added to the overall coziness, ensuring a restful night's sleep.
neg_text:
One of the disappointments was the value for money concerning toiletries. The limited selection provided in the room did not align with the hotel's pricing. Guests typically expect a more extensive array of toiletries, and this aspect felt lacking in comparison to other establishments.

Another inconvenience was the absence of an ironing board in the room. While the hotel may offer an iron upon request, having this basic amenity readily available would have added to the convenience of the stay.

The quality of the hairdryer provided was below expectations. A more professional-grade appliance would have been appreciated, as the one in the room seemed outdated and less effective than anticipated.

Furthermore, the inability to connect personal devices using an HDMI cable due to the unavailability of an adapter was a frustrating limitation. In an era where connectivity is crucial, this oversight could be a significant inconvenience for guests who rely on such amenities.

title: Great location but the hotel itself was a bit of a disappointment
pos_text: Very good location, nice and helpful staff, quite well soundproofed, comfortable bed.
neg_text: Very cold room: unable to adjust room temperature to suit your needs - unfortunately, despite increasing the heat level the temperature still remained the same - very cold. Blackout curtains. No wardrobe/instead of a wardrobe, a few hangers and 3 small, completely darkened shelves. The bathroom is literally in the room, separated only by a smoked glass window and a curtain, which means you can hear everything from there. The shower was open to the bathroom, literally flooding it, the water was so heavily chlorinated that the smell of chlorine lingered in the room for a very long time, and at the same time there was no way to air the room. No possibility to use the offer of streaming services despite the information provided on Booking.com

title: A Beautiful Object with Wasted Potential
pos_text: Location, decor, architecture of the facility
neg_text:
Beautiful facility and location, but I have the impression that everyone works there as a punishment. Well, maybe apart from the lady at the reception who welcomed us and the gentleman from room service. The organization in the restaurant is completely broken. Every second thing was missing at breakfast, the staff was wandering around the room with grimacing faces, not noticing that basic products were missing from the buffet. Drawing attention causes reactions, but with great grace, and even at that moment there is no smile on the faces. The preparation time for a meal from the breakfast menu was so long that we lost our hunger.

I would like to emphasize that the problem does not concern one waiter or waitress. The atmosphere was as if someone had just been killed, and what's more, it was probably the hotel guests who were the killers.

If a fish rots from the head down, then the head is in a state of complete decomposition.

Hotels in large cities fall into two categories.
1. It was great, next time I won't look for anything else.
2. It was okay, but I definitely won't be back. but how to do it to remember it.

This is the second category.

NOTE: The rooms have bathroom architecture that requires a couple traveling together to be in a state of mind where they can use the toilet next to each other. This may be a problem for some.

Aaa... and to this day I don't know what it means"The poached eggs are gone but they are still fried" in the context of a meal made fresh and ordered from the menu. What ended there? Water in the pot? Well, it's an egg if it can be fried.

When I asked for an extra duvet, I was told, "I could give it to you, but we're fully booked and the manager would have to work extra." When I was informed about the broken hairdryer in the room, I was told that it "should work" and only after being assured that it didn't work was a spare one issued to me. It spoiled my overall view of how we spent the entire trip. The heating/air conditioning was not working, but I didn't even report it after the previous negative experiences after contacting the service. The radiator in the bathroom kept turning off and we had a hard time drying anything on it. The floor, although covered with tiles, must not have been thoroughly cleaned before our visit, because after a few walks on it in white socks, they needed washing, and we didn't walk around the room in shoes.

title: good hotel only for one night because the next days you forgot
pos_text: the position
neg_text:
the hotel's philosophy (ADD IT TO THE SITE) which, under the pretext of adhering to an environmentalist plan, did not tidy up, clean or change the linen for four days (even though the website states daily cleaning). On the third day, in the evening, when I asked the reason, at reception I was told that the cleaning of the room and the change of bathroom linen (two shower towels and one hand towel for two people), although included in the price, was only upon request due to the hotel's participation in an energy saving plan and that they would take care of it the following day (they hand-delivered me two shower towels and only one hand towel which was to be used by two people). The next day (the fourth) the room was neither cleaned nor tidied and following my complaints to the floor cleaner, the room was cleaned and tidied in the late afternoon. These features of the hotel services (absurd because I can understand the change of linen, but what energy savings does the tidying of the room refer to?). Uncomfortable bed with springs that are barely supportive.
The staff at the reception, never a greeting or a smile

title: Okay for a short stay!
pos_text:
The hotel has modern furnishings. The rooms are also functional. The beds are comfortable, but very worn out.
Free parking is available in front of the hotel, if one is free. We had parked on the other side of the Oder river.
neg_text:
The hotel staff are young, but not very hardworking. Many people showed a lack of enthusiasm for work, and they also left their friendliness at home.
The breakfast buffet was no longer fully replenished even one hour before the end. The staff had to be reminded about many different missing items (e.g., cups). It was also unpleasant that, for example, spilled jam wasn't removed from the buffet!
Dinner consisted of a starter and a main course. This is quite a surprise, as there was no map to be seen anywhere!
On the first evening we were informed that water, apple juice and/or orange juice would be included at no extra charge. On the second evening, we were simply given water, and on the third evening there was nothing at all. We weren't even asked!
It was also strange that the restaurant was closed on Sundays. When we inquired about dinner, we were informed that dinner would be provided for guests who had already booked half-board. When we sat at the table, a small salad was placed in front of us. When asked again if that was all, the answer was that there were no waiters today and that this was all that was available in the kitchen. And actually, nobody really knows for sure.

title: Two banks
pos_text: Location, good breakfast
neg_text: Of the several hotels in Wrocław on the selected date, Dikul was chosen. Of course, I was guided by the photos of the rooms and I didn't even notice that there were no photos of the bathroom anywhere. Location, cleanliness in the room, breakfast ok. Unfortunately, after entering the room, consternation and surprise because the factual situation and photos are two different stories. The room was dark, as if it had come straight from the communist era, and the bathroom looked like a hospital bathroom from the communist era, the only thing missing was a white stool on metal legs. The nice lady at the reception told me that unfortunately all the rooms look like this, including the apartment offered. Yes, I think the photos were generated by AI or the owner's wishful thinking. I know there is competition on the market, etc., but I believe that honesty towards customers comes first. I suggest posting real photos of the rooms and bathrooms so that all customers can make an informed decision and not be unpleasantly surprised on the spot.

The latter is not a bad thing in itself, however, apparently all rooms are like that except for a few that are pictured on the portal. However, it was not clear to us which category they belonged to when we selected our rooms. Our booked king-size bed turned out to be two single beds pushed together with hard mattresses and rustling bedwetting protectors…
Considering the room price, I found the parking fee of €15 per night to be relatively high.

title: It's much nicer in Wrocław!
pos_text: The location is great!
neg_text:
It serves its purpose but lacks a feel-good factor and has several drawbacks!
We were allowed to change rooms because the first one smelled very rotten from the siphon. The rooms generally have the charm of a hospital room (everything is wheelchair accessible) - but they do NOT correspond to the photos on Booking.com. No housekeeping during your stay!
Dust was constantly falling from the ventilation system above the bed. The toilet brush had already seen better days. The refrigerator only works when you are in the room! Few electrical outlets and no bedside table for the person sleeping outside. TV only had Polish channels - unfortunately, battery acid leaked from the remote control!
There is a massive rat infestation in the neighboring building (rear entrance to the hotel and parking lot)!

The breakfast was okay, but it was the same thing every day or every other day, which was a shame.
Mattresses very soft.
Just FYI, no German TV, but that didn't bother us.

title: A disappointment, but in a good location
pos_text: ...a truly excellent location. Hassle-free luggage storage before and after your stay (generally the staff was rather nice and helpful). The breakfasts smelled nice but we didn't buy them so it's hard to say for sure. It's nice that there's a patio behind the hotel that you can use
neg_text:
The patio is clearly not monitored (or the curfew is not enforced) because throughout our stay we were disturbed by loud conversations and music until very late hours.
For information, the review is for a double room with a shared bed.
To start with we were given a card and a key. Strange and not very convenient, the card only turns on the electricity in the room, the key only allows you to enter through the door.
Upon check-in, there was no sign of cleaning in the room, except for the bed being made. There was hair everywhere (definitely not ours), dust, some lint, etc. The black grout in the bathroom also suggests that this was not a one-time problem. Additionally, room service is available for an additional fee, what about longer stays?
The lack of amenities also resulted in the scarcity of soap and bath liquid (in other words, the lack of any disposable cosmetics, which are standard), just 1.5 rolls of cheap and short toilet paper.
In the bathroom, the lighting was also partially not working and there was no option to turn off the terribly loud fan. The tap in the sink only worked with a motion sensor and this was rather inconvenient and generally seems like an absurd choice in a hotel.
The bed was not very comfortable, low and terribly small. My feet were sticking out from under it and the width was more like a large one, not a small two.
They even skimped on a second desk chair or an ice tray for the freezer.
The appearance, of course, depends on taste, but we didn't like it. So unkempt, it looked like some old student dormitory.
Overall I don't recommend it, but...

title: Great location and that's all, what a pity.

neg_text: Unfortunately, we didn't like the room, we didn't feel comfortable there and we wouldn't recommend it to anyone. It was obvious that his glory days were behind him. The bathroom in particular, although relatively clean, is very worn and needs renovation. The bedspreads were torn and stained in places. After turning on the blinds with the remote control, we were covered in a cloud of dust; it seemed no one had been there for a long time. At the promotional price, it's worth it for one night, but I definitely wouldn't stay longer.

title: Don't go to this hotel in winter 🥶
pos_text:
The hotel's location is close to the center, a few minutes' walk away.
Attractive building from the outside
neg_text:
Internally The hotel is cold, it's reminiscent of an administrative building, like a hospital. Cold, therefore, in a figurative sense, but especially, as far as we are concerned, in a literal sense… We arrived around 10 pm and at the time we didn't realize it, but our room was NOT HEATED… even though the outside temperatures were below freezing…. Incredible but true… I couldn't take a shower. I found the radiators were at 0 so I turned the radiators on and the air conditioning on heating mode. My wife went to bed fully dressed; she caught a cold. It was after 2.5 hours that it warmed up (the ceilings are very high, the windows too and it was unbearable, I was only able to take a shower around 3 am). I went to point it out afterwards at reception and I had to insist that the person take note and pass on my complaint.
The next day I went to reception to see the manager but they just gave me a form to fill out with the manager's email address…. I complained to reception, however, and all they could think to say was that she slept with the window open at night…. As if I cared to know… I am very disappointed and do not recommend this hotel, except in spring and summer.
Nothing there is warm, literally or figuratively.

Comfortable sofa but dirty as if from "mucus" was a bit scary. In the bathroom, the curtain is hanging on some cable… very poor quality soap. There is no TV on the wall facing the bedroom (not a problem) but there were cables hanging there and some "modem/decoder" that we kept hitting. At minus 8, you could heat up the room before checking in. Ladies at the reception sitting on their phones. It was relatively clean, there was an iron and a board to work on - that's a plus. The lack of tea despite having a kitchenette is another of many disadvantages. I feel cheated regarding the photos on booking.com. It was my husband's birthday weekend and I felt embarrassed by the situation.

title: Horrible experience, would never come again
pos_text:
The hotel is located away from the city center, which means it's pretty quiet outside.
The room itself is pretty large, nicely decorated and clean.
neg_text: There is a serious problem with internal sound proofing of the entire hotel. Seems like the air ducts from all the rooms are directly interconnected between them and also directly to the hotel lobby. That meant that, even though our room was on the 2nd floor (!!) and 50 meters away from the lobby (!!), we could clearly (!!) hear what people were talking inside the hotel lobby, up until 2 AM in the morning and starting again from 7AM, thus we couldn't properly rest. This could've been mitigated properly if the staff would've answered our initial booking message in which we specifically asked if they have problems with sound insulation and if they did, we could've cancelled our booking 4 months in advance. But they didn't respond at all. Furthermore, when confronted with the sound issues and the fact that we didn't sleep during the night and that we want to leave immediately, the staff didn't handle the situation promptly and wasted more of our time. We had to go through 2 managers and 3 rounds of explanations before they finally understood that we were not planning on staying another night and accepted to refund the remaining of the stay.

Bathroom - inspection by the cleaning staff supervisor is necessary - the shower cubicle is uncleaned, the grout, tiles, and the faucet underneath have 3-week-old residue. This is not for a 4-star hotel, but for a 1-star one. It is unacceptable for a guest to enter the large room with a buffet breakfast. This is what happened on October 20, 2024. The lady with the dog leaned over every dish displayed on the displays/counters, the dog was snorting "adorably," the lady touched every shared cutlery used by subsequent guests to serve food, then she put her hands under the dog's butt. It's hard to objectively call the hotel a four-star hotel - maybe it once met the requirements, today it's an average three-star hotel. The decor in the photos looks great, but in reality the hotel is worn, torn and cracked. As a host, I would be ashamed to provide guests with a room where the shower didn't work - it was impossible to set the shower handle at the right level above the head because it kept sliding down - the mechanism is probably x years old. The door to the room had a large gap between the frame, which intensified the audibility of sounds from the corridor. In the room we found a used capsule in the coffee machine, dirty water with limescale in the kettle, and dirty spoons, which effectively discouraged us from using this equipment. No iron in the room. The hair dryer is a relic of old swimming pools, it only dries short hair (men's), drying women's hair would probably take 3 days. The air conditioning was unbearably loud, it would have been impossible to sleep with it on - we decided to open the windows instead, which would probably have been impossible on the weekend due to the noise from the courtyard (the restaurant below the windows). The elevator smelled like carpet that had never been washed.
The hotel is simply begging for renovation.

We visited this hotel for Christmas, but unfortunately, our stay there ruined the holiday.

On the day of our arrival, the hotel’s kitchen stopped working at 6:00 PM, so we couldn’t order dinner.
This seemed strange, as the hotel had been constantly sending me spam emails about their Christmas menu.

There was no drinking water in the room; instead, there was an empty bottle on the table. The minibar was also empty.
When we raised this issue, the hotel staff rudely told us that we should fetch water ourselves from a cooler on the floor.
As for the empty minibar, they claimed that "this is the hotel’s policy."

The room was cold at night, even though we set the heater to maximum.
The bathroom and toilet had no heating at all, so we had to leave the door ajar to ensure a comfortable temperature by morning.

Breakfasts were mediocre and repetitive.
The carpet in the elevator was dirty, and they didn’t clean it during our entire stay.

Finally, the cherry on top: when we were checking out, the hotel attempted to charge us double for breakfasts.
So, be careful – this might be their usual practice.

Then at breakfast, the rather young and seemingly friendly personnel was running around clueless. They didn't see when things were running empty at the buffet. pos_text: The breakfast was extraordinarily good! The hotel is relatively close to the center, public transportation is in front of the hotel, but you can also walk to the center in 20 minutes.
neg_text: Misery. Dirty toilet, dirty bathroom door, with traces of dirt, hands or something greasy on it, black mold stains on the tiles next to the bathtub, the interior wall of the room was dirty, scratched and with torn wallpaper, old TV, old furniture, we didn't receive slippers.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The behavior and enthusiasm of the lady who checked us in.

The request for a quiet room was granted.

A sauna that we could use later than theoretically possible.
neg_text:
Mold in the bathroom (in both rooms we stayed in).

One of the rooms was very dusty, in the morning when I was moving the cupboard I could see my fingernail.

We asked the gentleman on the night shift for an anti-allergy pillow. He said he didn't know where they were, he would look for them and call them. He didn't call.

At around 400 PLN per night I think you can find better options.

title: Not a 4 star hotel
pos_text: The location, the staff, and the moderate price
neg_text: The accommodation is outdated. Our room had noticeable damp patches on the walls, the sink was cracked, the towel hooks in the bathroom were broken and not replaced, and the TV was not smart. TV function etc. It was also not entirely clean (older beard stubble in the bathroom). We would have expected a higher standard from a hotel in the Scandic hotel chain.

title: I'll go back there
pos_text: Scandinavian standards, breakfast
neg_text: Old, unregulated bathtub taps, either cold or super hot water.

title: It was my first stay in a hotel and I felt like I was in a staff room, not a guest room.
pos_text: Great location in the city center, close to everywhere
neg_text: The room's decor: peeling wallpaper and streaks, lots of mirrors, and the hum of a running fan in the bathroom. Weak TV receiver, missing most Polish channels.

title: I rate the stay at 4 because of the breakfast. this would be lower.
pos_text: Breakfast ok.
neg_text: TV. Not all stations are available. They were all supposed to be stationary. Decor in some rooms from the 90s.

title: Passable
neg_text: Very poor breakfast and coffee

title: The room is average in terms of equipment and furniture. The furniture is heavily used, the sink is cracked, the mattresses are worn out. Nice staff - breakfast
pos_text: The breakfast was great.
neg_text: Room

title: Nice design
pos_text:
I do not recommend a room facing the street. The tram stop right outside the hotel is certainly a plus, but for sensitive people it may pose a sleeping problem. Maybe rooms facing the street should be equipped with earplugs.
Too soft mattresses also did not help to rest.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: parking for disabled people with a parking card paid!!!

title: Passable
pos_text: Abundant breakfast with good variety
neg_text: The rooms are too old, they must be over 20 years old. This hotel is a former Marriott. I worked for Marriott in Rome at the Sheraton on Viale del Patti and this room is the same as the ones we had before the renovation.

title: Passable

Lokalizacja
Parking
neg_text: Standard hotelu nie zasługuje na taką ilość gwiazdek

neg_text: W mojej opinii pokoj, który otrzymałem wymaga już odświeżenia zwłaszcza jeżeli chodzi o meble i tapety.

title: Could be better.
pos_text: Good location near city centre and short walk to train station. Great variety of breakfast items.
neg_text:
Was charged for early check in- didn’t know about this, would have spent that money to stay elsewhere instead. Hard not to check in early when coming from the other side of the world.
Safe not working, had to get a technician to sort out. No signage to say the air conditioning won’t work if the windows are not closed properly. Dated room, strange layout to the bathroom. Toilet flush didn’t work at times.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Lokalizacja, obsługa
neg_text: Stan i czystość łazienki (grzyb, brudna toaleta)

title: Pleasant
pos_text: bardzo dobre i urozmaicone śniadanie.
neg_text: Otrzymałam pokój od strony ulicy. Stare okna w żaden sposób nie zabezpieczają gosci przed hałasem. Przed hotelem znajduje się bardzo ruchliwa ulica z torowiskiem i hałas z tramwajów jest bardzo uciążliwy w pokoju.

title: The price is inadequate to the standard
pos_text: Clean, very nice service :) and great location in the center 😌😌
neg_text: The price is inadequate to the standard of the room, for this money you can stay overnight in a beautifully finished room, and here it is a bit "old-fashioned" 😕 I probably won't come back, not for that money, for a room like that with a view of the pipes and ventilation.... Definitely not for people who appreciate a nice view in the room like me 🙁

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Instead of one large bed, there were single beds pushed together that kept moving apart, and the sink drain was clogged and the staff did nothing to fix it.

title: Ok to visit Wroclaw for 2-3 days
pos_text:
The location is close to the train station and the historic center. You can do everything on foot!
Breakfast is very varied, there is something for everyone.
neg_text: The bedding is uncomfortable, the interior insulation needs to be reviewed and the shower is also without pressure. The shower wasn't clean enough for my liking.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Good location, nice staff
neg_text: We booked a standard room, but upon arrival we were given a room adapted for people with disabilities, even though we had not requested this. Unfortunately, instead of making the stay easier, the solutions used made it significantly more difficult. The biggest disappointment was the bathroom – it was in poor technical and visual condition, which negatively affected comfort. Additionally, the television was mainly focused on foreign guests, which meant that access to Polish-language channels was limited.

title: Low price performance
pos_text: Good location not far from the train station and a short walk to the center
neg_text: Air conditioner did not work unfortunately and the high weekend price

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Świetna lokalizacja, w porządku śniadania. Stosunek jakości do ceny na plus.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Breakfast wasn't the best tbh
neg_text: Reception staff so helpful

title: Passable
pos_text: good location, very nice staff
neg_text: ugly room, socket only on one side of the bed, expensive coffee from the vending machine in the lobby - overall, the standard is so-so for the price paid.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Very good breakfasts, large selection, everyone will find something for themselves. Friendly and helpful service. The rooms and bathroom are clean. It is possible to rent bikes (it's a pity that there are only 4 and they are booked for the whole day, not for a few hours - so other hotel clients cannot use them, e.g. In the evening)
neg_text: Paid parking, bathrooms and rooms need refreshing, bathroom had poor ventilation (it was steamy for a long time), hotel more 3* than 4*

title: If the hotel improves, we will come back.
pos_text: The location is very good.
neg_text: The beds were very bad; the mattresses shifted with every movement. The bathroom was dirty and the fixtures were in need of renovation.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Good breakfast, fresh juices.
neg_text: No heating in the room

title: Charm of a youth hostel/Hostel
pos_text: The central location and the parking facilities.
neg_text: The room furnishings are very outdated. The beds are unusually narrow and very low, so they might be uncomfortable for older visitors. The rooms do not have conventional showers, but only bathtubs equipped with a shower screen. The hotel staff, hotel guests and service are more reminiscent of a youth hostel/hostel than a good hotel from the SCANDIC group. A considerable amount of work is needed to achieve a good minimum standard. My next stay in Wrocław will definitely be in a more "modern" hotel.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: We liked the hotel's location, so this was not our first stay at this facility. However, there is a huge gap between standard and standard plus rooms. We didn't expect it, because in other hotels, the difference is maybe only in the size of the room, coffee machine, etc. And here, as if they were two different hotels. We had previously stayed in a standard plus room and it was nice and modern. This time we chose a standard room and it was as if we had gone back in time 20-30 years. Fungus on the grout around the bathtub, weak water pressure in the shower, wallpaper in the room old, dirty, and sticking out. Unvacuumed floor.
neg_text: Mold in the bathroom, old, dirty, peeling wallpaper. Not very clean.

title: Specyficzne menu sniadaniowe chyba tylko dla gosci azjatyckich jedyna wedlina wyschnieta tragedia
pos_text: Lokalizacja
neg_text:
Tragiczne lazienki z wannami
Tragiczne sniadanie
Dziwne rozliczanie parking
Cena sniadania bez zadnego pokrycia w jakosci i asortymencie
Wyglada na hotel robotniczy z 4 gwiazdkami. Ale za co???

title: Not up to 4-star standards.
pos_text: The location was really good. Close to the city center, restaurants and public transport.
neg_text:
This is not a 4-star hotel. I have stayed at Scandic hotels in several countries, and the standard here was significantly lower than what I am used to. The room was very dusty. My partner is allergic to dust, and he had a very bad reaction whenever we spent more than two hours in the room. When we moved things around (such as taking the cover off the bed or hanging a towel to dry), you could clearly see dust in the air. The room also had a fitted carpet, which was not very clean either.

We stayed from Thursday to Sunday and were informed that there would be no cleaning or towel changes unless we specifically requested it. This is not what I would expect from a 4-star hotel.

title: I'd rather not come back
pos_text: Location ok breakfast ok service ok
neg_text: The hotel needs refreshing, the rooms are showing signs of age, we personally didn't like the soft mattresses, the rooms facing the tram tracks are a disaster, there's a loud noise all night long.

title: Would not reccomend this hotel.
pos_text: Breakfast was too pricey. Not told before hand that you had to let them know a day ahead if you were eating breakfast in the hotel. We were charged an extra 10 pl as a result. Location was fine.
neg_text: Staff not very friendly or knowledgable about things to do in the area or where places were. They would not replenish the room coffee and tea packets daily. You had to pay for coffee in the hotel lobby even though you were a guest. The laundry service was supposed to take 24hrs. Took 3 days to get out laundry back and the cost was expensive. Tried to get some currency exchanged for smaller denominations but the front desk wouldnt do it. Oir room phone wasnt working properly so the sent up a technition but he couldnt fix it and we never saw him again. All in all, I wouldnt reccomend the Scandic Hotel.

title: Tired rooms and poor housekeeping
pos_text: The location is ok
neg_text: Mattresses need replacing, no shampoo in the bathroom for two days :-(

title: In Spain it does not reach three stars
pos_text: Breakfast was the only good thing.
neg_text:
Room that leaves much to be desired for a 4-star hotel. In three nights, they haven't cleaned or changed the towels even once. Toilet paper isn't even sold that rough in Chinese stores. There are no amenities, the gel, shampoo, soap and a neck cream with a horrible smell all anchored to the wall, that's all there is.
Staff, yes, very attentive.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Breakfast is tasty and varied
neg_text: Bedding (poor pillows, small, crumpled), in the bathroom there is mold on the tiles and grout near the bathtub.

title: First and last stay at this hotel group.
pos_text: Friendly personal
neg_text: Room not clean, damaged, mold in bath, extreme noise from tramway in front of hotel, old fruit and bread at breakfast.

title: It's good that the stay was only two nights.
pos_text: Adequate sized room with decent furnishings
neg_text: The whole room reeked of cigarettes, probably from the bathroom ventilation. This made all our clothes smell like cigarettes. We are non-smokers and this strongly discouraged us from staying at this hotel. Additionally, the room itself is showing its age and this is visible in many of the decor elements. You also get the feeling that the material elements are not very clean. Additionally, we stayed overnight on New Year's Eve and loud music could be heard all night long - I don't know if it was from the hotel itself or if the hotel is connected to residential buildings and that's where the noise came from.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Good location, reasonable prices, friendly staff.
neg_text:
During our stay, there was construction work taking place in the hotel on our floor/in the elevator. We heard the noise from 9:00 am onwards, so waking up was quite unpleasant.
Even in the evening when we returned to the hotel, there was still drilling and hammering going on not far from our room.

title: Passable
neg_text: Jedzenie w restauracji jest poniżej krytyki. Makaron podany jest nie z sosem, ale z jakimś rzadkim płynem z rozgotowanymi pomidorami. Sandacz nie ma smaku ryby i powoduje rozstrój żołądka. Poziom kuchni to nie 5 gwiazdek, tylko żenada.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: A good, varied breakfast
neg_text: Poor TV quality and few programs, burnt out bulbs in lamps, overall the hotel needs renovation.

title: Passable
pos_text: Location and service - nice, helpful, professional, young people. Sauna - also open especially at our own request and possibility of storing luggage after check-out.
neg_text: The room was small, and it was not of a standard that would differ positively from other hotels, including smaller ones. You could even say it gave a feeling of the last decade. Even though we chose a "standard plus" room. Old TV with limited channel selection. The cleanliness left much to be desired. The air conditioning from the restaurant next door made it impossible to sleep at night. Greenwashing - a lot of emphasis on big words about ecology - a bit too much, because this ecology is not really visible in the hotel. We understand that breakfasts are subject to a fee, but espresso coffee and water can be provided. It's nothing special, but it makes a big difference.

title: Passable

title: Passable

neg_text: Pokój od strony ruchliwej ulicy z tramwajami, karetkami i motocyklami powinien mieć stopery na wyposażeniu. Bez stoperów nie da się w takim pokoju spać. Obsługa dopiero po trzeciej prośbie zaczęła szukać innego pokoju.

title: Für eine Nacht ok

neg_text:
Nur eine Bettdecke
Zimmer zur Straße raus, zu laut, durch die Tram und Autos

title: Pleasant

Es gibt einen Parkplatz direkt beim Hotel, der allerdings extra kostet.
Innenstadt ist fußläufig zu erreichen.
neg_text: Wir hatten ein Zimmer zur Straße. Das war bei geöffnetem Fenster sehr laut (mehrere Straßenbahnlinien).

title: Breslau bei anderer Hotelwahl ein Muss für jeden Urlauber.
pos_text: Die Lage des Hotels war sehr gut. Bis zur Altstadt waren es zu Fuß nur wenige Gehminuten. Das Frühstück war für Gäste ohne Lebensmittelunverträglichkeiten sehr gut.
neg_text:
Das Frühstück für Gäste mit Glutenunverträglichkeit hätte ansprechender gestalten werden können. Es gab jeden Tag das selbe Brötchen. Eine andere Auswahl oder beispielsweise Cornflakes hätten den Tag angenehmer beginnen lassen können.
Uns wurde im Verlauf der Buchung ein ruhiges DZ mit Dusche versprochen. Die Nächte haben wir in einem unruhigen Zimmer der 6. Etage gegenüber dem Fahrstuhl verbracht. Es war nur eine Badewanne vorhanden. Wir hatten ein Doppelbett gebucht, die Betten standen auseinander. Auf dem Fensterbrett begnügten sich mehrere Tauben. Das benötigt keiner weiteren Worte. Geweckt wurden wir fast jeden Zag durch sehr zeitige Hotelanlieferungen.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location Breakfast
neg_text: Street noise is very disturbing.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: breakfast
neg_text: not very clean, standard room on the 6th floor in relation to the price

title: Setback
pos_text: The location
neg_text: The hotel is well located, but the interior is old and in dire need of replacing. The whole hotel is dark and doesn’t look clean. The fitness room and sauna were always out of order. In addition, the room will not be cleaned for stays longer than one night.

title: Pleasant
