title: I recommend it, but there are some drawbacks
pos_text:
The best advantage of the hotel is breakfast. The price for breakfast is quite high, but worth it all. The breakfasts were very good, fresh and there was everything you could want: granola, various types of yogurt, muesli, milk, fresh fruit, juicer, fruit juices, scrambled eggs, eggs, bacon, baked vegetables, omelets, cheese, blue and blue cheeses, olives, various salads, cold cuts, butter, coffee, tea. Vegetarian zone: vegan cheese, cold cuts, cheese, plant-based sausages, plant-based milk. Chia, tapioca. For coffees, teas, ginger, lemon, white sugar, brown sugar, sweeteners. Chocolate cream, maple syrup. Still, sparkling, cold water. Really… there is everything. Additionally, there is the option of breakfast boxes, which are really very filling. There are also highchairs for children in the breakfast restaurant, so that's very nice. Reception. This is also a huge plus. Nice people and in fact, they solve every problem and whim. We wanted a non-slip mat, we got it. We wanted a candle, we got it. And other things too, which brings us to another advantage… Housekeeping. The maids are wonderful and do a good job. We asked that all glass items and decorations be removed from the room before arrival, as we have small children and we did not want them damaged. And everything was hidden and secured. When cleaning was ordered, it was obvious that it was clean and quick. Women really do make it. Eco-friendly hotel. There are waste sorting bins in the rooms. They do not clean rooms every day, only upon request. At breakfast they make smoothies from the remaining fruit. All products are eco and healthy. Additionally, you can take coffee to go with breakfast. They offer paper cups, which is a huge plus, because you usually can't take anything out of the kitchen. There is a small “shop” in the lobby where you can buy everything you need. Tampons, pads, razor, shampoo, body lotion, and snacks + coffee machine. Prices are high, but things are needed.
The Stockholm restaurant, although open at low hours, has nice service. Delicious drinks. Soundproof rooms.
neg_text:
-There was a huge luggage trolley at the entrance to the hotel, but no one offered to take our luggage, even though we had a lot of luggage.
-At the very bottom there is the Stockholm restaurant, which is unfortunately open from Tuesday to Saturday and from 5 p.m. This is very problematic because breakfast is until 10:00, so I have to wait until 17:00 for dinner.
-Fitness room (gym and sauna) is located on the first floor, next to the conference rooms. To get there you have to go through the corridor through all the conference rooms. Which may be uncomfortable when you want to go, for example, to the gym already dressed.
Hotel Room (standard family room)
-The bathroom is missing a single light. When you want to use the toilet. e.g. at night, all the big lights come on at once.
-The bathroom lacks a towel hook, e.g. to your hands so that it is at hand.
-The bathroom has a bathtub with the option of a shower. There is a glass pane that is placed in an unfortunate place because you cannot turn on the water through the glass.
-The basket in the bathroom is very small.
-There is a bin in the room for sorting, but it is very small. With 4 people in the room, we had to ask for extra garbage disposal every day because we didn't fit.
-We had one large bed and one sofa bed. This sofa was a disaster. It's scary for an adult to sit there, let alone sleep, because every time they turn around, the whole thing moves and squeaks. It's impossible to sleep.
-A 4* hotel should have room service as standard, but unfortunately it doesn't. What a pity.
-The fridge is very small and not normal, it only keeps the temperature a little.
-There is no WI-FI on the TV, you can't start e.g. Netflix. Just regular television.
-The carpet is so colored that it appears very dirty.
-The air conditioning worked as it should and was very loud. Additionally, it was written that the air conditioning would turn off automatically when the window was opened - this did not happen.

title: Wonderful hotel with a beautiful view from the window and parking!!
pos_text:
Wonderful hotel, very friendly staff, we needed to check in late in the evening and the hotel was always in touch with us.

We stayed with a child under one year old, and although the hotel provides a crib upon request, we did not need it, as the child sleeps with us. I was very pleased with the size of the bed, where the three of us fit perfectly (even taking into account that the child slept across). A pleasant bonus was also the toys that the staff prepared and left in the room in advance so that the child had something to do.

Beautiful views of the old town from the window, a large number of restaurants to suit every taste and budget in the area, and delivery directly to the hotel is also possible; the girls at the reception will pick up your order and notify you.

And of course, the most important thing for traveling by your own transport is that the hotel has parking available, which is a huge plus since the hotel is located in the very center. The price is 50 zlotys per night, which is cheaper than other hotels nearby.

We will definitely choose this hotel again if we are in Wroclaw, thank you very much!

title: Would not stay again.. many other accomodations in better locations/value for money
pos_text:
The old building that has been renovated to create this hotel.
The staff were friendly, one lady was particularly helpful (the lady on front desk the next morning after our arrival)
neg_text:
Many things.
* BIG CONCERN.. we had ordered an Uber one morning for 6:30am pick up. When we arrived down stairs and waited inside for the Uber to arrive, we realised the front door was locked (large wooden doors). There was no button to open them or any information how to open them. I become quite panicked and was trying everything to get outside. It was a terrible feeling to feel trapped with no idea of how to get out.
It was then a young lady came and unlocked the door with a large key.
(Big concern as there are no fire evacuation instructions or any emergency instructions (not in polish or English to be seen anywhere).

* Definitely not catered for the English speaking traveler (not one tv channel catered for in English speaking, not even the ability to use subtitles)
* Location: while in an okay location it is NOT on the old town market square. It is on the very outer of the old town and about 5 large blocks (walking) to the actual market square.
* Two sachets of tea, two sachets of coffee left for one week staying at this property. NO milk. When asked for Milk they said they did not have any. We had to buy our own.
* House cleaning is at an extra charge (we were fortunate to get 1 free clean due to the length of our stay. This should be mentioned in their profile.
* As the room was not serviced daily the dirt and dust and hair built up quickly on the floorboards. It become quite dirty to walk around in. A broom and dustpan would have been useful. The rooms are large and a lot of floor space.
*No hand towels left and had to use the bath mat to wipe hands on. Towels were old and ratty on the ends.
* When asking for One extra pillow, they said they didn’t know if they had any??? 24 hours later they delivered one.

We booked 7nights stay and left on day 5. We realised there were far better value for money stays, closer to the market square and with better facilities/amenities.

In the evening it turned out that the hotel parking lot was not full and my car would have fit easily. Sewer odors in the bathroom are an additional attraction. I do not recommend, avoid this place.

A very pleasant stay at the Radisson Blu in Wrocław. The hotel benefits from an excellent location, close to the city centre, which makes exploring very easy. The rooms are clean and comfortable, and the overall atmosphere is relaxing and well-organized.
The breakfast is varied and of very good quality, with fresh options for all tastes. In addition, the staff is extremely friendly and professional, contributing to a very pleasant experience. I highly recommend this hotel for a city break or a business trip.

A hotel that is really worth recommending. The Radisson Blu in Wrocław has an excellent location, very close to the center, which makes the city easy to explore. The rooms are clean, comfortable and well maintained, and the atmosphere is very pleasant.
The breakfast is varied and tasty – perfect to start the day with energy. In addition, the staff is extremely kind and attentive, always willing to help.
In short: very good location, cleanliness, excellent service and an experience that makes you want to come back. This is exactly how a 5-star hotel should be. ✨

Dar ceea ce face acest loc cu adevărat special este personalul – amabil, profesionist și mereu cu zâmbetul pe buze. Este genul de hotel din care pleci cu gândul că ai găsit locul perfect pentru următoarea vizită în Wrocław.

Only my bank was able to get the money back one month later. Due to illness, we were only able to stay 2 of 5 nights, and there was no compensation for 2 rooms and parking fees that had already been paid for. I have never experienced anything like this in 30 years and 55 countries I have traveled to!

neg_text:
The room is tiny and has "tenants" in the form of ants crawling around the bathroom and the room. After reporting the issue, the room was not changed, but "cleaned". On the same day in the evening, a similar situation occurred, i.e. the "tenants" returned. We were promised a room change the next day when another room became available. The next day in the afternoon we were given a room change but this time on the noisy side facing the main street towards the center, the windows seemed to be missing, the noise and traffic seemed to be constantly open. Sleeping pillows are like flat "pancakes", so sleeping problems and neck pain are guaranteed. The breakfast was very poor and meager, and the cleanliness was questionable. The bathroom had a bathtub the size of a baby's bathtub, and there was no socket in the bathroom, so you couldn't plug in your curling iron or hairdryer. The TV cannot connect to the internet, so if you have streaming services, forget about using them.
The hotel parking lot is a joke, tight, small, and the worst part is that if you buy a space and go out into the city, you can't be sure there will be a space for you when you return. Parking is prohibited on the streets around and the city guard is often present. THE HOTEL IS A FAILURE, WE WILL DEFINITELY NEVER GO BACK THERE.

The owner defended himself by claiming that these were “illustrative photos” and there may be a difference, but for me it is a scam and misleading guests. I always choose my hotel very carefully and this is the first time I have had such a terrible experience. The bed has a torn bottom, the pillows have been washed a million times so much that the filling is made of compacted oakum and is impossible to sleep on, the towels are gray, hard, and frayed, there are no bedside lamps or sockets by the beds, and the iron is burnt out. The only positive thing, after the intervention of the booking.com customer service office: after filing a complaint, I got some of my money back. However, what I ended up paying still absolutely does not match the standard I slept in. Three stars is a misunderstanding.

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
My adventure with the Radisson Blu Hotel Wrocław began like a good novel – with the first sentence that completely captivated me. The very sight of the hotel, discreetly nestled between the greenery of Słowacki Park and the monumental architecture of the Racławice Panorama, promised history on a grand scale. And he kept his word.


This was no ordinary stay. It was a 5 senses experience.


Sight? I was delighted with the elegance of the interiors – modernity flirting with the classic, light dancing on marble surfaces, a space that breathes peace.

Hearing? I was resting – even though the center of Wrocław was pulsating with life, the silence in the room was so perfect, as if someone had muted the world with a remote control.

Smell? Freshly brewed coffee in the morning led me straight to the Aquarelle restaurant, where breakfast was more of a celebration than a meal.

Taste? Poetry. Every detail of the buffet is refined like a verse in a sonata.

Touch? Perfectly soft bedding that made me briefly consider extending my stay…for a week.


But the real heart of this place is the people. The service was not "professional". She was attentive. Warm. Authentic. The kind that remembers what coffee you drink and asks if the city fulfills your dreams. I felt not like a guest—like the welcome hero of this story.


In the evening, a walk along the Oder River, a few steps from the hotel, had something cinematic about it. I returned to my room, looked at the illuminated Wrocław and thought that there are places worth visiting… and there are some to which we return.


The Radisson Blu in Wrocław is not a hotel.

It's a stage where every guest plays a leading role.

And me? I'm already booking an encore.


⭐ 10/10 — for elegance without pomp, luxury without coldness and memories that stay long after check-out.

title: An unpleasant experience
pos_text: Location. The location is absolutely ok.
neg_text: In the room we were given, the bed had broken boards. It's a shame that no one did anything about it when the room was being prepared before my arrival, and it was certainly visible after removing the bedding. We only noticed it when we went to bed, but since it was late we decided we would somehow get through the night on that bed. The next morning I reported the bed to the reception. I asked for a room change. Unfortunately, the lady at the reception said she couldn't change the room because the hotel was 100% booked, so I suggested changing the hotel. The lady said she would contact the hotel manager and call him, and she noted the bed failure. After reporting, we left the hotel. No one called during the day, so we assumed that perhaps the bed had been repaired or replaced, or that a place in another room had become available. Imagine our surprise when we returned to the hotel in the evening and found that nothing had been done about our case. The bed was just as broken before we left as it was when we returned. My second intervention at the reception desk and a conversation with the manager resulted in the bed being replaced. Of course, when I first reported it at the reception desk, the lady didn't inform anyone about it, let alone the manager. However, the hotel showed no sense of guilt, no compensation - not even in the form of a free breakfast, not even a simple apology from the receptionist who ignored the matter. I know that 3 stars is not 5, but still, some standard is expected from a 3-star hotel, especially in such an important matter. On my part, there is still a bad taste in my mouth, I will definitely avoid this hotel on future trips.

title: Exceptional
pos_text:
One of the best hotels I've ever stayed in.
Amazing location, in the city center, next to the river, yet a very quiet street, not noisy whatsoever! Next to lots of public transport stops and the bus from the airport.

Very clean, very modern, comfortable rooms with everything you might need. The beds are super comfortable.

The room is "smart", you can control the TV, lights, temple, set an alarm, contact reception and many more things via it. You can even screencast your phone to the TV.

The breafast is great, doesn't vary day to day but there is enough variety to not get bored and everything was delicious.

Free coffee, tee, hot chocolate and apples, all dag and night, which is a great bonus.

Lots of useful services at the hotel, they can even get you a PlayStation if you request it (didn't try that but it was in the hotel info).

Didn't try the restaurant but it was priced very decently.

Very kind and helpful staff.

Overall, just an amazing experience and I'd love to come back!
neg_text:
The only thing is that I am not a fan of the "open" bathrooms with glass but they offer a curtain to cover this part of the room so you can have some privacy.

title: Dziecko-widmo
pos_text: Lokalizacja
neg_text: Jestem bardzo rozczarowana pobytem. Na szczęście był krótki. Wybór podyktowany lokalizacją i to chyba jedyny atut tego miejsca. Śniadanie bardzo przeciętne (smak i wybór), w połowie jego trwania połowa dań się kończy i nie jest już uzupełniana, ekspres psujący się, wielokrotnie wzywana była do niego obsługa. Podejście hotelu do dzieci karygodne. Rezerwacja dokonana przez Booking dla 2 dorosłych i 9-letniego dziecka. Zaproponowano nam pokój z dużym podwójnym łóżkiem i na takie się zdecydowaliśmy z pełną świadomością, że będziemy spać we trójkę. Natomiast nie spodziewaliśmy się, że zostaniemy potraktowani, jakby dziecka z nami nie było (oczywiście poza podpisaniem odpowiednich oświadczeń dot. małoletnich). Już w recepcji dziecko zostało usunięte z rezerwacji, ponieważ ograniczenia systemowe nie pozwalały na zameldowanie. W pokoju wszystko przygotowane dla dwóch osób: dwie poduszki, dwa ręczniki, dwa kubki na herbatę itd. Gdy poprosiliśmy o dodatkową poduszkę dla dziecka, bo jednak głowy mamy trzy i trudno to obejść to usłyszeliśmy, że hotel ma pełne obłożenie i nie mają dodatkowych poduszek. Gdyby były wolne pokoje to obsługa przyniosłaby poduszkę stamtąd, ale niestety, nic nie mają. Kierownik niedostępny, nawet telefonicznie. Dowiedzieliśmy się też, że to nie pierwsza taka sytuacja, personel obsługujący zgłasza podobne sprawy wyżej, jednak niczego to nie zmienia. A ściany windy obklejone ulotkami promującymi ekologię, przyjazność dla dzieci i zwierząt, na samym środku zasady generalne opisujące standardy chroniące dzieci, np. "Każdy gość hotelu, w tym dziecko, powinien być traktowany z należytym szacunkiem i poszanowaniem jego godności". Tymczasem dzieci w hotelu są niewidzialne. Czysta hipokryzja. Serdecznie nie polecam.

title: Exceptional
pos_text: I heartily recommend it. The hotel, the atmosphere, the staff, the breakfast - everything is at the highest level. The vintage cars from the entrance made a huge impression on us. We were with a child in the pre-Christmas period, and at the entrance we were warmly and family-friendly greeted by the hotel's President himself in a wonderful, colorful Christmas suit - our little one, despite being tired, was delighted. The gentleman at the reception is also a wonderful, very nice man. Breakfast was not buffet style due to the period (the day before Christmas Eve), but it really exceeded our expectations - we received a package for two people, which, despite good intentions and great hunger, was impossible to finish. Here, too, we were met with the heart of the President and his staff, as our son woke up with a fever and everyone rose to the challenge to improve his well-being. Thank you very much for this short but wonderful, warm, family-like and pleasant stay. When in Wrocław, we will definitely come back here.

title: Windows that don't open, noisy airflow and stuffy air at night
pos_text: If you have a car, there is good transport connection to the Old Town (10-15 minutes via the Warsaw Bridge). Nearby there is a KFC restaurant and a BP gas station. Hotel guests can use the small swimming pool free of charge, although only during specific hours (during our stay it was from 4:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m.).
neg_text:
- The biggest disadvantage is the non-opening windows (probably to save on fire insurance) and an unreliable ventilation system that malfunctions if other guests on the same floor have their ventilation options set incorrectly. This caused quite a stuffy atmosphere on the first night, with hot air pouring in from the unopened window and the room temperature ranging between 25 and 32 degrees Celsius. C. The loud airflow turned off by itself after 2-3 minutes. It was already quiet at night and the facility staff were unable to check the settings in other guests' rooms. As a result of the intervention, we received an office fan and a promise that it would be repaired the next morning. To survive until morning, we had to sleep with the door ajar, risking having our luggage stolen.

- Extra paid parking, parking at the BP gas station is risky (only allowed up to max. 30 minutes under threat of towing).
- Equipment such as a kettle, glasses, cutlery or garbage bags are not standard, but you can easily ask for them at the reception.
- The pool is a bit small, designed for a maximum of 6 people. Then it gets too crowded and competitive swimming, especially crawl, becomes impossible for fear of hitting other guests.

- Breakfast additionally payable PLN 45 - standard offer without fireworks, fine for max. 3 mornings. Then, due to the repetition of the offer, monotony creeps in. There are no large plates, so you have to go to the buffet several times with small plates.

title: Passable

The receptionists were not very pleasant and when I reported a problem with the air conditioning, the lady came in with obvious dissatisfaction.

2)The pillows provided were half-empty and incredibly uncomfortable, and there were no others in the room.
The duvets they provide are too heavy even with the air conditioning off and you end up sleeping half uncovered or sleeping in your underwear and feeling cold or in your pajamas and sweating.

3)Cleanliness needs to be reviewed, the lamp above the bathroom mirror had a layer of dust that had accumulated who knows how long ago.

4)More seriously, we stayed for three nights and they failed to clean the room every day, with the result that on the last day we had neither clean tablecloths nor toilet paper rolls.

5) the air conditioner was too powerful and even setting it to 22 degrees (the minimum) meant you were suffocating after a very short time and the lack of an opening window was a problem.

I understand that it is a conference hotel but here we don't even feel the passing grade.

title: Nigdy tam nie wrócimy.

neg_text:
Zacznę od czegoś co pozostawiło niesmak na cały pobyt. Przebyliśmy w wakacje cztery miasta, Pragę, Bratysławę, Kraków i Wrocław. Każdy hotel na tym samym poziomie, (min ****) niestety tylko w booking.com. tak to wygladalo w rzeczywistosci HP park plaza nie dorasta do pięt pozostałym.
Pierwsza rzecz (i nie chodzi o koszty bo było to tylko 160 zl) trzeba było dopłacić za pościel dziecka i ręczniki mimo że rezerwacja była na 3osoby. Niby nic ale żenada pozostanie. Nie wiem czy zawinił @booking.com czy sam hotel.
Pozatym hotel już wymaga remontu. Jak większość starych hoteli które "jada" na opini z przed 20lat. Takie mieliśmy wrażenie po wejściu do pokoju... podróż w czasie 20lat. Wystrój trudno, każdy może mieć inny gust, ale meble już nie działają jak powinny, naprawy w łazience na "łapu capu" (silikon pokrywał grzyba, a on na złość i tak wychodził bokiem), po prysznicu stróżka wody przez łazienkę!!
Obsługa była na niskim poziomie. Nie mówię że mają się uśmiechać cały czas, choć w innych hotelach problemu z tym nie mają. Na pytanie o funkcjonalnosc parkingu i jak wyjeżdżać kazdego dnia było tylko "burczenie" pod nosem.
Może problem tkwi w nas że nie jesteśmy Niemcami czy tez obywatelami zachodniej Europy. Przyglądając się z boku widać było różnice w obsłudze Polaków i Niemców.
Napewno będziemy omijać szerokim łukiem ten hotel.

title: Das Verhalten des Hotelpersonals war äußerst unangenehm und wenig sympathisch.
pos_text: Nichts hat besonders gefallen – vom Service bis zur Ausstattung war alles mehr als schlecht
neg_text:
Der Service war inakzeptabel – als Gast fühlte man sich weder willkommen noch ernst genommen.
Bei auftretenden Problemen wurde nicht lösungsorientiert reagiert. Stattdessen wurde Druck ausgeübt, um berechtigte Kritik zu unterdrücken. Leider betraf dieses Verhalten nicht nur eine einzelne Person, sondern war bei mehreren Mitarbeitenden zu beobachten.

Das Personal zeigt keine Bereitschaft, kundenorientiert auf Probleme einzugehen. Statt aktiver Unterstützung entstand der Eindruck, dass man sich hinter der Rezeption versteckte.

Besonders unverständlich war die Änderung meiner Buchung:
Obwohl ich ausdrücklich ein Zimmer mit Frühstück reserviert hatte, wurde mir ein kleineres Zimmer ohne Frühstück zugewiesen – ohne Angabe von Gründen. Aufgrund fehlender Englischkenntnisse beim Personal war es zudem nicht möglich, eine nachvollziehbare Erklärung einzufordern. Stattdessen wurde mit Aussagen wie „Wir haben kein Zimmer für Sie!“ oder „Wir können keine zusätzliche Bettwäsche anbieten – das geht nicht!“ reagiert – selbst in Fällen, in denen ich bereits für die gewünschte Leistung bezahlt habe.

Aufgrund der ursprünglich viel zu kleinen Zimmer für eine Familie war ein Zimmerwechsel vor Ort notwendig verbunden mit einer zusätzlichen Zahlung. Selbst dieser organisatorische Vorgang gestaltete sich äußerst kompliziert: Die Aussagen der Mitarbeitenden widersprachen sich erheblich, sodass der gesamte Prozess nervenaufreibend war.

Bereits bei einfachen Bitten, wie der Bitte, den Frühstückstisch abzuwischen, da noch deutlich sichtbare Spuren vom vorherigen Gast und Haare vorhanden waren, zeigte sich das Personal unaufmerksam. Erst nach mehrmaligem Nachfragen wurde die Bitte halbherzig erfüllt – freundlich war der Umgangston dabei keineswegs.

Besonders irritierend war schließlich, dass man nach dem Aufenthalt eine separate Nachricht vom Hotel erhielt – mit der Bitte um eine gute Bewertung.

Das Frühstück war unspektakulär – die vielen Top-Bewertungen kann nicht nachvollziehen!

pos_text: During a two-day stay at this hotel, the first day was ok, the service was very nice. Towels are changed regularly at the customer's request, cleaning products, coffee, tea and toilet paper are provided.
neg_text: During my second night at the hotel, in a rented apartment, I experienced an unpleasant adventure. Namely, in the morning while sleeping my bed collapsed (cornea) on which I slept when the children slept on the double bed. I immediately informed the hotel staff about this, expressed my dissatisfaction with this situation, asking for an appropriate approach to the customer in such a situation, taking into account the quite high cost of renting the apartment. After informing the hotel staff, I was informed that the owner of the facility would be informed about this situation and that the facility's cleaner would inspect the bed. Unfortunately, after 6 a.m., I was forced to return to the apartment without the possibility of further rest. On the floor where my apartment was located, a cleaning lady was working around 9 o'clock and I asked her to inspect the bed, which had collapsed. After a long time, after the cleaning lady had inspected it, I managed to put the damaged hinge from the extendable part of the cornea back in place and it was possible to fold it or unfold it again. At approximately 9:15 a.m., I was informed by the hotel staff at the reception that, after speaking with the owner, I would be charged PLN 400 for damage to the bed. During this conversation I also received information that the cornea is not intended for sleeping for adults. After informing the hotel staff that they would refer the case to a lawyer for treating me and my family in such a way, that a room with a damaged bed was rented for me and my family, endangering my health which I still feel in my lower back and that I had to pay for the repair of the already damaged bed. After this conversation, after a dozen or so minutes, I was informed by the hotel staff that the hotel owner would not want to charge me for the cost of repairing the damaged cornea.

title: Bad experience
neg_text:
We noticed that the central heating wasn't working. I called the reception to inform them. They sent a man to fix it. He informed us that it started working properly now, but that we shouldn't have it on full speed because it gives cold air (????) and he set it on low speed. Half an hour later the air coming from the a/c was cold. I called again the reception to let them know, and the girl of the reception told me that her colleague informed her that it will take some time and we should wait 15-30 minutes. Half an hour later I went down to the reception, asked the girl to go and check it on her self that the air coming it's cold. She insisted that she just spoke again with her colleague which informed her that the room's temperature just started increasing and it will be warmer and warmer.
After we spent a couple of hours in the room and waited the temperature to get warmer, we asked them to change us the room. They said that they don't have another room and they brought us a small fan heater. The bathroom was cold throughout the whole night, the fan heater was very very noise and we had to close it too at night, leaving the room very cold.
The worst part is that I informed them that this is going to be a bad review for their hotel and they couldn't care less. They lied from the beginning as they knew that their heating is having issues in all the building (I have read some reviews afterwards from other customers), they made us spend 2 hours inside the room to wait for the heating to start, even though we only had 1 day to see the city.
At the end we preferred to sleep in a cold room without the noise of the fan heater.
I do not recommend this hotel to anyone. Totally unprofessional people. The worst 150€ we spent on a single night.

title: A stay that somehow managed to be smooth, comfortable, and unexpectedly entertaining.
pos_text:
Every hotel stay blends into the next after a while. Same rooms, same breakfasts, same “it was lovely” reviews that mean absolutely nothing.

This one didn’t.

Not because of anything dramatic. Quite the opposite. It just quietly got everything right. Check-in was smooth, the room was exactly what you want after travelling, and nothing felt like a compromise. No noise, no faff, no “why is this like this?” moments.

But the highlight? Breakfast theatre.

On our second morning, a man arrived in full hotel robe, like he’d taken a wrong turn out of a spa and decided to commit to the look. Fair enough. Except he then proceeded to pace the lobby like a minor celebrity avoiding paparazzi, surveying the room with the kind of calm authority usually reserved for people who own islands.

Nobody stopped him. Nobody questioned it. And somehow, it worked. The whole place carried on as if this was completely normal behaviour.

That moment summed the hotel up better than anything else. Comfortable enough that people relax into it, but polished enough that even a bathrobe power-walk through reception doesn’t break the atmosphere.

Beyond the unexpected entertainment, everything else delivered exactly as it should. Great location for exploring Wrocław, genuinely good breakfast, and staff who manage to be helpful without feeling scripted.

It’s rare to find somewhere that just works without trying too hard.

We’ll be back. And next time, I might bring a robe just to see how far I can push it.
neg_text: The only minor issue was the free candy at reception. A great idea, unless you arrive with children who immediately identify it as a core feature of the hotel. From that point on, leaving or entering the building required a negotiation I was rarely winning.

The room is relatively clean, the furnishings are older, which is not a problem at all. The bed was a big disaster. We chose the double bed option, which would normally sleep three people. Unfortunately, it wasn't a double bed, but two single beds next to each other, the mattresses immediately moved apart when you lay down. There was an extra bed on top of the mattresses that was not suitable in size, which was probably supposed to connect the beds optically. On each side of the bed, the side part ended 20 cm earlier than the mattress, and there was a 15 cm gap between the mattresses. Absolute disaster.
But what was the absolute worst experience was the hotel restaurant. We decided to have dinner here. The food was okay, the service was slow, arrogant, and unfriendly. We didn't have anything to drink, when we ordered we got half of what we ordered and the highlight was that when we paid, when we announced 3 times in advance that we wanted to pay separately because there were more of us at the table, the waitress brought us the bill with a funeral stamp. I told her that I told her 3 times that we wanted to pay separately and that I wasn't interested, I was told what we wanted, that we could send the money. No apology for the mistake, still quite rude behavior. Really crazy!

The hotel facilities, common areas and rooms are in excellent condition. The truth is, the photos don't lie. The beds are comfortable and the shower is spacious, as is the bathroom. The location allows you to enjoy Wroclaw's nightlife without any problems, and during the day you can easily get away to other points of interest or simply explore its museums and historical sites.
The breakfast is EXCELLENT, it has VARIETY and it's FEELING. The food is excellent, and the service from the waiters, as well as the tableware used, is impeccable. The truth is, I was pleasantly surprised.

After reading the reviews, we decided to stay at this hotel, but unfortunately, it was a real disappointment. In our humble opinion, this property does not have the characteristics of a 4-star hotel; to be generous, I would only give it 3 stars. The furniture and bathrooms are very dated and the rooms are small. The reception staff was very rude both during check in and also when, the next day, we complained because at 4pm. 00 the rooms had not yet been cleaned. The girl at reception arrogantly said that they should prioritise cleaning the rooms needed for tourists checking in, without even apologising for the inconvenience. So if you want to take a shower or rest for an hour in the afternoon, it's better to change hotels. Cleaning only consisted of changing towels and making the bed in a cursory manner. We found the hair we left on the bathroom floor in the same place. The rooms were equipped with a coffee maker and a kettle. It's a shame that we only found the pods on the first day, from the second day onwards nothing could be done, the machines were only there for show. Breakfast left a lot to be desired. Not much selection in the sweets department. Every day only croissants and a small portion of cake. Only two types of jam and in small quantities, not enough for everyone. Nutella or other non-existent chocolate spread. Every day the same variety of savory dishes and bread, not always fresh that day. In short, a big disappointment, nothing to do with the
other 4-star hotels where we stayed in other cities in Poland.

There are no kettles in the rooms to boil tea, nor are there any available downstairs in the hotel lobby, and the mini bar is serviced by the lady at the reception, who will either come and serve you if she wants, or she won't. So if you want to warm up after a long day of walking around the city, I recommend a cup of tea at the KFC across the street.

The rude behavior of the receptionist, who acted as if we were bothering her, everything was a problem and information about the accommodation, e.g. How much and where is breakfast, info about the pool, etc. The highlight was the problem with parking, when the lady told me that they were full and that we should leave the car in a shopping mall about half an hour away on foot or at KFC (where they will tow you away after 2 hours). I explained to the lady that we had found a spot, that we were already standing there, and the cone (which indicates that the spot was occupied) was not there. About ten minutes later, a friend at another reception desk arranged it without any problems.

The pool was small and smelly. According to the instructions on paper and from another receptionist, we are not allowed to take towels from the room to the pool (which seemed logical and normal to me), they are supposedly available at the pool for a deposit. The pool staff completely ignored us and after we pulled him out of watching a video on his computer, he started yelling at us that he didn't have any towels, that maybe we had towels in our room, so what do we want from him?

The bar, which advertises itself as open 24 hours, was no longer staffed at 10 pm. The lady at the reception told us that no one was there, that she would try to call someone... after about half an hour of waiting, the receptionist came to pour us a drink.

Overall, we are absolutely appalled by this hotel, where every employee has different information, everyone is very unpleasant and nothing actually works. We will definitely not return here and I would definitely not recommend staying here to anyone.

Let me start by saying that there was a problem with the payment right from the start. While I was flying in the morning, the hotel had withdrawn the entire amount from my account when I had made the payment at the hotel. We later realized that this was a security measure, but this doesn't seem right to me, given that the agreements weren't like this, especially since no one ever told me that this was the procedure.
A few days earlier, they had already tried to withhold a similar, albeit lower, amount, and after requesting information through booking, the responses arrived, albeit rarely, but they weren't clear, and after a while, I didn't even receive a response.
In addition to this: toilet seat stained with urine NOT ours and even though I reported it as soon as I entered the room at reception they limited themselves to apologizing but no one cleaned it that same day or even the next day, I had to clean it myself.
The shower had no curtain or wall, directly on the floor, with the risk of a nearby hair dryer getting wet and causing serious injury. Towels were stained and few in number. We asked for face towels and were given three foot towels (I assume they were the bathmats with feet on them, as they're for the shower). Stained sheets and matching pillowcases were also included. The carpet on the floor was extremely dirty and inappropriate for such a space unless it was properly maintained. title: Holiday in a sanatorium
pos_text: Location partly
neg_text:
Unfortunately, the pictures, descriptions and reviews have nothing to do with what you actually find there.
The only great thing about the hotel is its location and the short walk to the city center and the market square. However, it's also noisy at night.

Otherwise, it resembles a sanatorium, or perhaps it was a retirement home.
The bed is so deep, it almost feels like sleeping on an air mattress. The room is large, so why are the beds so small? Only 1.40 meters wide!

Unfortunately, the bathroom is not a place where one feels comfortable at all. It's all age-appropriate, which is certainly helpful for some guests, but it has nothing to do with a nice bathroom in a hotel. The smell is unbearable and the towels are old, worn out and smell very unpleasant.

The elevator or the door is so extremely loud when opening that you can hear it all along the hallway.

The breakfast is good, but there is only one coffee machine, which also takes a very long time. Why not put up at least two? The breakfast room is small and too crowded; not everyone can find a seat. Unfortunately, it's also very uncomfortable.

All in all, the stay was unfortunately not relaxing. It was supposed to be a short break. The price is comparatively cheaper, but by no means justified. Half would have been more appropriate.

For us, this was our first and last stay there.

Przy pierwszym telefonie Pani z recepcji powiedziała, że zadzwoniłam za wcześnie i mam się kontaktować bliżej terminu przyjazdu. Przy drugim telefonie ponoć w tym samym czasie miał być Panieński i pani która odebrała nie chciała mi umówić masaży, bo „niewiadomo czy ten Panieński się odbędzie”. Poprosiłam, aby państwo się ze mną skontaktowali jak będą coś już wiedzieć. Oczywiście nikt do mnie nie zadzwonił. Przy 3 telefonie z mojej strony odebrał Pan który w sumie nic nie wiedział i kazał mi się umówić później. Dzwoniąc 4!!!!!! raz Pani powiedziała, że nie ma jeszcze grafików dla masażystów. Poprosiłam o kontakt. Tak, nikt do mnie nie zadzwonił 😂

Wchodząc do recepcji przywitała mnie nieprzyjemna Pani która odniosłam wrażenie, że bardzo nie lubi swojej pracy i mnie jako człowieka. Rozmowa przy zameldowaniu była równie nieatrakcyjna jak powitanie owej Pani.

Kolejnym minusem jest to, że w godzinach 10-16 restauracja jest zamknięta i nie ma możliwości zamówić jakiegokolwiek jedzenia.

title: Passable
neg_text:
We stayed at this hotel for the weekend with a friend and we specifically chose it because of the relaxation area – jacuzzi and SPA, which were one of the main reasons for our booking. Unfortunately, the jacuzzi was not working for the entire two days of our stay. No one informed us about this in advance, and when we checked in they only said that "they will fix it tomorrow" – which did not happen.

The room was very cold and I would rate the cleanliness as 2/5 - definitely not at the level of a hotel that costs over PLN 600 per night. Reception service was also poor – 2/5. Upon check-out, we were not given change for 50 PLN, which I only noticed later.

The breakfasts were delicious – that's the only positive aspect of the stay.

We filed a complaint via Booking.com and received a response that the hotel could offer us… a voucher worth PLN 100 to be used for your next booking at the same facility. It is hard to consider it as taking the situation seriously – such a “gesture” after a completely inaccessible jacuzzi and numerous inconveniences it seems simply ridiculous.

We came with the intention of relaxing and unwinding in the wellness area, but we had to look for entertainment outside the hotel. If we had known that it would not be possible to enjoy a holiday of this standard, we would have definitely chosen a cheaper hotel – this one was absolutely not worth the price.

pos_text: The hotel is nice because it is stylish and nicely restored, and that is where the positives of this hotel end
neg_text: Employee approach to customer service. The gentleman working at the reception and the lady behind the bar gave me the impression that they were working there as a punishment. On the first day we were given a room without windows but with the name Anna, just as we wanted. The bathroom seemed to be not cleaned and there were remnants of cosmetics from the previous owner, which is documented in the photos when I asked the receptionist to move or upgrade to another room. He said that these rooms were not available. I was very surprised because the rooms were still available on booking.com, but due to the late hour, we didn't make much of a fuss. Suddenly the next day another employee upgraded us without any problem. When we wanted to use the bar, the lady said the bar was closed, and the gentleman at the reception who checked us in also said the same, even though there were people sitting inside.30 minutes later the bar was suddenly open and I could order a beer when I saw other guests ordering too. Work as punishment? It might be worth training employees. On the first evening we reported a problem with hot water because after one person took a bath it turned out that there was no hot water. The next morning the same problem also appeared and we reported the problem to the reception. On the second night after moving to another room, the problem with the water continued. Sometimes it was hot, sometimes it wasn't. We reported it several times to the reception desk, everyone pretended that everything was fine. The problem only resolved on Monday after check-out, when the receptionist admitted that they were right that they had a problem with the hot water. It's a shame that for two days, none of the employees were able to notice that cold water was running from the tap in every room. The pool area was very nice, but there was no one to ask about massages or additional spa services. Apparently this is a spa and wellness hotel?

title: Excellent hotel in the center of Wroclaw
pos_text:
Everything was great! The hotel is clean. The room was very quiet (I wrote to the hotel in advance to ask for a room with windows facing the courtyard). They checked us in quickly and very politely. The room was newly renovated, everything was very clean and tidy. The bed linen and towels are snow-white. The mattresses are high and very comfortable. Mon was of good quality.

A hair dryer with a narrow nozzle is just what you need for styling your hair. TV with Netflix and Youtube.
The curtains are absolutely super - they darken the room, so you can get a great night's sleep.
Breakfasts are fresh. There was enough of everything, even if you came towards the end of breakfast. The coffee is excellent, the juices are not powdered, they are normal. All the food was fresh.

There were a lot of guests at the hotel, mostly groups from European countries, but in the corridors and rooms there was no noise or conversations from neighbors at all.

The location of the hotel is very convenient. In the center. The Market Square is a 10-15 minute walk away.

I recommend the hotel. We liked everything!
Thanks to the team of the Campanille Prime Hotel - Wroclaw Stare Miasto. When we come to Wroclaw, we will choose this hotel again!

title: Never again…
pos_text:
There was a tram stop nearby.
The bed was very comfortable and it was relatively clean.
neg_text: Hotel two days before arrival (date is early December) he sent information that they had a special occasion - a company Christmas Eve party. If I were to change the location, hotel prices at that time for such a close date cost at least PLN 500 per night (ignoring the problem of availability on weekends) - I come to Wrocław alone to study. I arrived before 10pm, tired after a full day of education. I think I spent over 20 minutes at the reception, even though I had already provided all the information on booking - invoice details, phone number, and I don't know what else the lady wrote there. I say they already have my number, she says she doesn't have a number. And the hotel called me 2 hours earlier... Typing speed slower than slow. She was supposed to send me the invoice by email right away. The music was blaring beneath me all night long, and the receptionist refused to move my room to a quieter area, even though there was a place on the opposite side of the room. At 4:30 I was woken up by the screams of a couple making love. And I don't think they were very loud at all, but the walls are very thin. A great night as before the next morning classes, waking up at 6:30. The bathroom was recently renovated, but the shower head was probably 20 years old. Hair stuck to the shower walls. But only 4. As if that wasn't enough, the hotel doesn't know how to issue invoices. I waited almost 3 weeks. There was another problem because they were supposedly issuing a correction for a week because someone made a mistake. They responded to messages with a delay of several days. And the atmosphere is a bit like the PRL. All together: a tragedy in its entirety.

title: I will definitely come back here if there are any available dates, unfortunately there are no dates in December…

title: Very good
pos_text:
The hotel is generally ok, without any wow factor, but it serves its purpose. I would rate it about 8/10 considering the price.

The hotel itself is not new, you can feel it: The interior and equipment are already slightly outdated, in some places there is a characteristic "age" smell - both in the rooms and at the entrance to the building. Nothing critical, but an update would definitely help.

The main and very significant advantage is the presence of a swimming pool and sauna right in the hotel. This is a really big bonus: You don't need to go anywhere, you can calmly swim and relax in the sauna. It is precisely this convenience that makes the hotel a winner.

The breakfast is quite tasty and good value for money – nothing fancy, but filling and enjoyable.

The location is approximately 30 minutes from the city centre, which is worth considering when planning. Overall, this is a standard hotel, suitable for a short stay or if the on-site pool and sauna are important.

pos_text: Overall, a nice hotel and my rating would be completely positive if it weren't for a coincidence. I arrived in Wrocław in the evening on the day when the Polish national football team was playing a match against Portugal. When I was going to Wrocław, I was hoping that I would be able to watch the match in the hotel - on the TV in the room or at least in the hotel restaurant. It turned out that there were no TVP channels in the entire hotel, so there was no way to watch the match. Let us add that this hotel is somehow connected with the Śląsk Wrocław football club! I ended up having to go to the city center (it's about a 20-minute tram ride) and find a pub there, where I watched most of the match - because unfortunately I didn't make it in time for the start. Unfortunately, the bad taste remained - plus additional night trips around the city in the rain, which I had not planned.

The staff at this hotel are very good.
Nice, friendly and smiling.

I am very pleased with such a beautiful and unique place.

title: If I may advise, for your stay in Wroclaw, definitely choose another hotel for the sake of your health.
neg_text:
Upon arriving at the hotel, I was very unpleasantly surprised that the window in the room could not be opened. The temperature in the room was about 25 degrees. After asking at the reception, I was told that this was to prevent guests from jumping out of the window and that I should use the air conditioning. I was unable to adjust the air conditioning to lower the temperature, instead it reached 30 degrees within a few minutes. After further inquiry at the reception, I was told that the air conditioning cannot be set to cooling during the winter months, only to heating. When I asked (in English) how I could lower the temperature in the room, as it was impossible to sleep peacefully at 28 degrees, the receptionist replied that we were in Poland, and therefore she would only speak Polish to me. However, within the next few sentences, she smoothly switched to Ukrainian, probably because I couldn't understand her. Still, she didn't give me any advice.
I find the receptionist's approach completely unprofessional and discouraging.
She is probably employed there to discourage guests from asking unpleasant questions at the reception.
The sleep at night was very restless, it was impossible to breathe peacefully, and in the morning I woke up with a severe headache. There was no question of rest.
When I realize that the room has no ventilation, and the air in the room has certainly been the same for the last few years, I can be glad that I didn't end up with some serious problem in the care of healthcare professionals. This can be a very serious problem for allergy sufferers.
I have stayed in about 16 hotels across Europe over the last two years, but the experience at this hotel is clearly the worst.

Po dłuższej rozmowie zaproponowała, żebym zarezerwował pokój przez booking i zapłacił kartą, wtedy się uda (tak zrobiłem). Przy wystawianiu faktury, w recepcji był pan i powiedział, że da się wystawić jedną fakturę bez wcześniejszej opłaty przez booking, tylko przy meldowaniu muszę wybrać z menu co będę jadł i od razu to doliczą... I moved out before five o'clock, sleep-deprived and angry. As compensation, the hotel offered me... 10% discount on your next stay. Yes, sure - I will definitely use it :D

The quality of customer service is evidenced by the fact that in response to my written complaint, the hotel manager did not even bother to sign it, so I can only deduce her name from the email address. I didn't recognize the name.

For the first time I see a top-class hotel unable to solve the problem of two young people who mistook a place to rest for a student dormitory. This is the first time I've seen a hotel even ashamed of its manager's name.

PS The air conditioning is very noisy (maybe it has not been serviced?).

title: Very overrated 5*, it's max 3*
pos_text: The only advantage is the location
neg_text: We do not recommend this hotel, 5* is only on the front door. Old rooms, shabby furniture, dirty walls, outdated air conditioning system, no individual heating in the bathroom, no radiator or heated floor, cold bathroom. The standard room is a much lower standard than the same rooms in other hotels in Wrocław with fewer stars. Water dispensers on every floor, too bad there wasn't sparkling water. Breakfast is a joke, poor selection, no live cooking and often present at lower standards. Dried out salads, cheeses or cold cuts. The service does not replenish if something is missing. In every other Radisson we have been to, not only in Poland, the standard was great, but here it is a maximum of 3*. There is a separate lounge for Radisson Rewards members, as well as the top two floors, also for members only. I believe that in a 5* hotel everyone should be treated like a prince, not just the chosen few. The only advantage is the location.

title: Stay with obstacles
pos_text:
The breakfast was plentiful and good. The location is very central. Just a few hundred meters to the market square.
The room was very large, as was the bathroom, and both were very clean. The heating worked well too.
neg_text:
The key card didn't work. The parking garage, which had spaces for hotel guests, was located on a one-way street where the hotel was also situated. However, the parking garage was a few meters in front of the hotel, so you couldn't turn around and drive straight in, but had to go forward through half the city and make a loop to get into the one-way street. For me, it happened at night and in the rain, so it was very tiring. Then there were several entrances to parking garages next to each other. The description of the parking lot was only in Polish. I encountered several cars reversing out of the various underground parking garages, all looking for the right spot, just like me. The whole thing took me almost 2 hours. It was said to be a secure parking garage, but I didn't even need the key because everything was always open.
Then I decided to stay another day and asked if there was a room available, perhaps even mine? The receptionist said no. Only one with a lower standard, with only one bed. I said it was no problem, since I was traveling alone anyway. Then she said that I would have to check out of my current room and could only check back in at 3 pm, and that I would have to wait until then. That made no sense to me, of course, as I wanted to see the city during the day. There was absolutely no willingness to store my suitcases for that long. Overall, both receptionists were neither friendly nor helpful.

title: Long wait at check-in
pos_text: Comfortable room, excellent breakfast
neg_text:
1. I arrived at the hotel at 12 noon on the day of arrival. I was asked at reception to wait until 3 pm. Only then would check-in be possible and the room ready for occupancy.
Okay, I came back at 2 pm after a walk through the city. Once again, I was told to come back at 3 pm.

Is it really too much to ask that rooms be prepared a little earlier and not be rigidly insisted upon by the fixed time?

So I had to wait another hour in the lobby until the room was finally ready. Then only after repeated requests at reception. The journey had been tiring, and all they wanted to do was relax a bit in their room.

It might only take 20 minutes to prepare a single room.

The organization is poor in this regard.
I will arrive after 3 pm on my next visit.

The Scandic is not some fly-by-night hotel, but a large, reputable hotel.

2. When I checked out, the young man at reception wanted to charge me separately for breakfast, even though it was already included in the package and I had already paid. The error has been resolved. The employee did not apologize, which I had hoped for.

Overall, I had the impression that Polish guests were treated more kindly and courteously than Germans.

title: It is necessary to add sun loungers or at least chairs by the pool, control the water temperature and the operation of the sauna.
pos_text: Excellent breakfasts for a 4* hotel and location.
neg_text:
I booked this hotel solely for the pool, gym and sauna.
On the day of arrival, they initially refused to give me a robe, citing the fact that the hotel was overbooked and all the robes had already been taken for today, so come back tomorrow. then they said that I had a cheap room, since it was for one person. ( I don't understand anything here at all, is it cheaper because it's for one person) or don't all room categories include a robe in a hotel with a pool?
An hour later, another extremely nice young man, without any questions, arranged for two robes to be delivered to my room in 15 minutes.
But, as it turned out, that day the pool had very cold water, and the sauna was practically not heating. The gym was completely closed. There were 3 sun loungers, which is very few. On the second day, the pool became a little warmer, but still uncomfortable, and the sauna was even colder. One sun lounger was already broken and there were 2 left. Then I just got sick, I assume it was because of the hypothermia in their “spa” and for the third day I didn’t go anywhere, and there was no point.
Also, on the second day, there were no towels in the locker room at this spa and there was no one at the spa reception desk to ask for anything.
If you don't need all that, it's a normal average hotel in the city center with good breakfasts.

title: I absolutely do not recommend - terrible value for money
pos_text: The only positive thing is the varied and tasty breakfast.
neg_text:
Firstly, the aesthetics of the corridors - the carpet, probably burned by chemicals, looked like it was from a cheap hostel. Horror.
Secondly - an elevator with scratched handrails and worn-out instructions according to which one must use the key card before choosing the floor; no markings on which floor the elevator stops.
Thirdly, information - after booking, when calling and asking about parking options - I was informed that the parking lot was 350 m away and cost PLN 120, so I organised parking in a different way. When checking in, it turned out that there was another parking lot available for PLN 40 (I did not receive this information when I asked during the phone call).
Fourth - cleaning products in the bathroom. I have no complaints about the general cleanliness, but the products used in the bathroom caused shoes to stick uncomfortably to the floor.
Fifthly - a nice lighting effect in the bathroom - the wall was made of glass doors leading to a balcony shared by several rooms, but .... matte foil on one of the windows was carelessly glued, which meant that when standing on the balcony in the evening, with the bathroom light on - the person standing on the balcony (I remind you of the shared balcony) has an excellent view of the person using the bathroom and unless you are an exhibitionist, this is very uncomfortable.
I won't add that when asked at the pool whether there are towels available, the answer is "if they're not hanging there, then there aren't any."

I had one of the older rooms with very unappealing rugs on the floor and a toilet that was in need of repair. There was also an awful recurrent noise-maybe from a heating generator or something else, but it was disturbing. There was hair at the bottom of the egg dish at breakfast, which rather put me off what otherwise looked like a decent breakfast. And while I appreciated the pool and sauna and made use of them, there were no towels in the spa area nor any offer to get some. No bathrobes in the room to compensate for the lack of towels either. The hotel has a superb location in Wroclaw and has potential to be such a great place, but in its current state I can’t recommend it at all.

pos_text: Location - close to the old town
neg_text:
1. I asked to reserve a parking space - I received an answer that they cannot reserve it, but it costs PLN 110 per day.
2. After entering the room, I decided I would like a beer - I looked into the bar - empty. The lady at the reception explained that due to the pandemic, they removed drinks from the mini-bar and will probably not return them - company policy.
3. If there's no beer, I'll drink water, whatever. There is no water either, there is a carafe and a message to look for a dispenser in the corridor and pour some. Apparently they want to be ecological and have given up using plastic. They could have given it in a glass bottle. Apparently Radisson's policy too.
4. And to top it all off, there's a cloth bag on the bed with a story about how to skip cleaning the room because it's eco-friendly.
RADISSON! I have a few more ideas for you: convince your customers not to flush or wash themselves, change bed linen and towels for every third customer - you will be even more eco-friendly!!!
5. The best was at the end. When checking out, I told the lady at the reception about the above observations, to which she replied that she fully understood me. When I said I was going to share this in my hotel review, she immediately offered me a discount for my stay and asked me not to. It seems that they are afraid of bad reviews, they have a prepared response to individual cases in the form of a bribe, but they do not intend to change it systematically.
To sum up, it is not worth paying 510 for this pseudo ***** hotel. I will do that next time

title: Нужно добавить лежаки или хоть стулья у бассейна, контролировать температуру воды и работу сауны.
pos_text: Прекрасные завтраки как для 4* и местоположение.
neg_text:
Я бронировала этот отель исключительно для того, чтоб иметь бассейн, спортзал и сауну.
В день заезда сначала мне отказали в халате, ссылаясь на то, что отель переполнен и на сегодня уже все халаты разобрали, приходите завтра. потом сказали, что у меня дешёвый номер, так как он на одного человека. ( тут я вообще ничего не поняла, так он и дешевле потому что на одного) или не всем категориям номеров положен халат в отеле с бассейном?
Через час, другой, максимально милый молодой человек, без каких либо вопросов, организовал мне 2 халата в номер за 15 минут.
Но, как оказалось, в тот день бассейн был с очень холодной водой, а сауна практически не грела. Спортзал вообще был закрыт. Лежаков было 3 шт, что очень мало. На второй день бассейн стал чуть теплее, но так же некомфортно, сауна ещё холоднее. Один лежак уже был сломан и их осталось 2. далее я просто заболела, предполагаю, что как раз из-за переохлаждения в их «спа» и третий день уже никуда не ходила, да и смысла не было.
Также на второй день в этом спа уже не было полотенец в раздевалке и постоянно отсутствовал человек на рецепции спа, у которого можно было бы что-то попросить.
Если вам это все не нужно, нормальный средний отель в центре города с хорошими завтраками.

It was by far the worst nights sleep I have ever had! Then the next morning I thought I would get up and go for breakfast and sit in the spa hoping my room would get cleaned and I could then go back to sleep … return back to room at 4pm to still not have been cleaned , had to call reception over-wise I don’t think it would of been done. Very disappointed with the whole place and will not be returning. Didn’t even get a message back from the hotel to acknowledge the whole situation or offer any sort of solution. Waited 24 hours for a message and didn’t get it so here I am … my advice for any one going to stay here find out if they are having a party etc and if they are do not stay there.
